programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Great CyberWar, the Doctor arrives in the far future, intent on protecting the last of the human race from the deadly Cybermen. But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Watford at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Norwich City at Molineux and Arsenal v Everton at Emirates Stadium. The stakes were high for all six teams in action, with Watford and Norwich fighting for survival and the other sides all hoping to play European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals in the top flight(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Catching up with Andrew and Laura Evans, who headed to Perth for a week in 2016, trying out the properties, jobs and lifestyle available should they choose to move there for good(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Anita Manning value items brought to Blackburn Cathedral, while Paul Martin learns about how mill workers in the town used to be woken by a human alarm clock(n)

programme.title: Winterwatch
programme.desc: Chris Packham, Michaela Strachan, Gillian Burke and Iolo Williams investigate rumours that swallows are present in Sussex in January. They also look at why there's been a severe lack of snow in Scotland this winter, and share an incredible spectacle in the form of flocks of brent geese migrating from Northern Canada to the urban streets of Dublin(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Walks of Life
programme.desc: JB Gill journeys along a 12-mile stretch of the River Severn in Shropshire to discover the ways civilisations have lived and worshipped along its banks since the dawn of man. Starting in the shadow of Ironbridge, he finds out how the family that constructed the bridge and kick-started the Industrial Revolution were guided by their Quaker faith. After a spot of coracling on the water, he stops at the ruins of Buildwas Abbey and meets a modern-day monk to find out how their penitent lifestyles have changed since the structure was in its prime. Later, JB visits the ruins of the Roman city of Wroxeter, before lending a hand at a local vineyard(n)

programme.title: World's Sneakiest Animals
programme.desc: Chris Packham features animals that utilise devious tactics to win a mate and raise a family, such as bower birds and fiddler crabs, who use optical illusions to impress a female, and male topi antelope, who fake danger to give them an edge. He also looks at family life, including the chicks that blackmail their parents, and the honeyguide bird that dupes another species into raising its young(n)

programme.title: Red Rock
programme.desc: Rory risks arousing suspicion when tempted to tamper with evidence, Sharon seeks an ally in her quest for the truth, while Keith risks walking into a trap(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians explore the life of the South Devon branch line before the Beeching cuts of the 1960s. Peter Ginn joins a footplate crew and receives coal-shovelling tips from a veteran steam engine driver of 63 years' experience, and with Alex he learns about one of Scotland's most lucrative exports. Ruth Goodman finds out about the dangers involved in working on the Victorian railways, and how those who lost limbs were catered for by a dedicated prosthetics workshop. She also discovers how the railways came to the rescue when a deadly disease wiped out almost the entire stock of London cattle(n)

programme.title: Blue Planet UK
programme.desc: A look at whether herring, the 'silver darlings of the sea' are making a comeback. Plus, a man shares his passion for sea horses and Gillian Burke investigates whether being a brave crab is a disastrous strategy(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Charles Hanson and Philip Serrell hunt for objects to take to auction at Blackburn Cathedral, Lancashire. Charles values a wooden Fijian club, while two vintage enamel signs catch Philip's eye. Presenter Paul Martin takes a look at the impressive Talbot collection - local press photographs taken of events, celebrities and the royal family over a 50-year period(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jamelia, Susan Calman, Gareth Malone and Paul Martin test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games, with the week's overall champion set to be crowned(n)

programme.title: Great Asian Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the island city-state of Singapore on the last leg of his 2,500-mile exploration of south-east Asia. In Tai Seng he marvels at the world's largest subterranean rail depot where self-driving MRT trains are operated. He then takes a cable car to the island of Sentosa where he learns about one of Britain's worst military defeats and the brutal Japanese occupation that followed. At the Singapore Stock Exchange, Michael appreciates the island's success as a financial centre and, in the residential district of Ang Mo Kio, visits an urban farm of the future - on a car park roof(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of the unpaid volunteers who sacrifice their free time to staff more than two hundred RNLI lifeboat stations across the UK. Matters they tend to on an ongoing basis include injured rock climbers and crashed paragliders(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Bobby Davro and songstress Sheila Ferguson of the Three Degrees compete to find profit-making antiques in Kent, guided by experts James Braxton and Catherine Southon. Away from the contest, Bobby hears the little-known history of the Royal Engineers, while Sheila discovers how black culture and music arrived - and thrived - in the Garden of England(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the Co Down coastline between Belfast and Strangford Lough, along the Ards Peninsula. In Belfast, Adrian road-tests the largest hydraulic crane in the world and helps park a 100-metre-long Dutch cargo ship. Mehreen goes kayaking on Strangford Lough to see how the currents of the UK's largest sea inlet is placing the area at the forefront of renewable energy research. Plus, Helen Skelton tests the waters of the North Channel, one of the most dangerous swims in the world, and local comedian Shane Todd visits a long-established bespoke racing-car factory(n)

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: The first of two compilations of highlights from the 17th series of the quiz with a difference, hosted by Sandi Toksvig. Guests including Tom Allen, Jo Brand, Susan Calman, Alan Carr, Jimmy Carr, Phill Jupitus, Nish Kumar, Prue Leith, Joe Lycett, Jason Manford, Sarah Millican, David Mitchell, Johnny Vegas, Holly Walsh and Josh Widdicombe joined regular panellist Alan Davies to answer questions based on subjects beginning with the letter Q(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Glasgow, calling in at a martial arts club in the east end and exploring the ruins of Crookston Castle. Frankie's home city is no stranger to riots and protest and he meets one high-profile campaigner who became famous as one of the 'Glasgow Girls'. On the journey to his stand-up show at the Gaiety Theatre in Ayr, Frankie pops into the town of Troon to meet some members of one of the most famous showmen families in Scotland. Upon reaching Ayr, he catches up with fellow performer Elaine C Smith to discuss performing onstage and their different styles of comedy(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events(n)

programme.title: Front Row Late
programme.desc: Mary Beard and her panel of guests explore the links between crime and culture. Together, they pick over why the detective novel is such a big part of the literary and theatrical landscape, as well as the wider responsibilities of writers who produce crime narratives. Moreover, they debate whether the cultural representation of crime is sometimes to blame for encouraging violence in real life(n)

programme.title: In the Heat of the Night (1967)
programme.desc: When a wealthy industrialist is murdered in Mississippi, the bigoted local sheriff is quick to accuse a black newcomer to town - only to learn his suspect is a highly respected homicide detective from Philadelphia. However, the duo are forced to put aside their mutual loathing and team up to track down the killer. Oscar-winning drama, starring Sidney Poitier, Rod Steiger and Warren Oates(n)

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: A Panorama investigation into the multinational company's rise to corporate superpower, asking whether there is a dark side to the public's love affair with the firm. Former high-level insiders reveal that its customer obsession has led to a huge data-gathering operation, enabling the company to use what it knows about us to shape not only the future of retail, but the workplace and technology too. The programme also hears from senior executives who say the company is a force for good(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Graham, Yasmin and Ryan join the Doctor on a trip back to 1816, where they visit the Villa Diodati on the shores of Lake Geneva as Lord Byron and the Shelleys plan a night telling ghost stories. Their aim is to spend the evening soaking up the atmosphere in the presence of some literary greats - but a supernatural presence is all too real and the Doctor is forced into a decision of earth-shattering proportions(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Naomi Wilkinson and Tim Warwood examine a play date with a polar bear, a dolphin that thinks it is a sperm whale, and the ducklings with a cat for a mum(n)

programme.title: The Dog Ate My Homework
programme.desc: Children's comedy panel show hosted by Iain Stirling, featuring studio-based frolics and slapstick challenges(n)

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: Eleven-year-old Maariya was scared of going underwater, but overcame her fear a few years ago(n)

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: Contemporary dance group Vale perform in the studio(n)

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve's search for deadly animals takes him and the crew to South Africa to observe the great white shark, where he gets some fantastic views of the 'king of the ocean' - and to demonstrate the team's hunting prowess, he has a special trick up his sleeve. Just as feared by many is the Cape Buffalo, known locally as the black-death. The presenter takes to the skies to track down a huge herd of this notoriously bad tempered bovine beast, before being charmed by the snake-stomping secretary bird(n)

programme.title: Tomorrow's Food
programme.desc: Comedian Dara O Briain and the team of experts examine more of the science transforming how food is grown, bought and eaten. Angela Hartnett meets a robot that can mimic all the moves of a leading chef, Shini Somara heads to Japan to discover if technology can beat willpower in the world of weight-loss and greengrocer Chris Bavin finds out if the British public will ever accept insects as an appetising snack. Plus, Dara discovers how forensic science is tackling the billion-pound business of food fraud(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The team discover how the combination of increased leisure time and affordable rail transport brought a new kind of freedom for working-class Victorians. Ruth Goodman travels along the south Devon coast from Paignton to Kingswear, where she helps prepare a paddle steamer for a journey up the River Dart, while at Swanage, Peter Ginn finds out what it was like to work on the excursion trains and the impact mass tourism had on the area(n)

programme.title: Our Coast
programme.desc: Adrian Chiles and Mehreen Baig explore the Co Down coastline between Belfast and Strangford Lough, along the Ards Peninsula. In Belfast, Adrian road-tests the largest hydraulic crane in the world and helps park a 100-metre-long Dutch cargo ship. Mehreen goes kayaking on Strangford Lough to see how the currents of the UK's largest sea inlet is placing the area at the forefront of renewable energy research. Plus, Helen Skelton tests the waters of the North Channel, one of the most dangerous swims in the world, and local comedian Shane Todd visits a long-established bespoke racing-car factory(n)

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Comfort Food
programme.desc: Si and Dave turn up the heat on family favourites, including roasted spiced cauliflower cheese and a Caribbean-inspired plate of jerk pork chops(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Wild Brazil
programme.desc: A fierce drought culminates in huge and ferocious fires, and the capuchin monkeys, giant otters and coatis must use their survival skills to endure the extreme conditions. The animals are also looking for mates so they can breed in time to ensure the next generation are born at the optimal moment(n)

programme.title: Flatpack Empire
programme.desc: Cameras follow Mia Lundstrom as she takes on IKEA's biggest challenge in a generation - pushing into India with an aggressive expansion plan. Not only is the company bringing their stores and products, but their unique culture and values. Meanwhile, staff at a Wembley branch create room-sets that better reflect a changing world(n)

programme.title: The World's Most Extraordinary Homes
programme.desc: Piers Taylor and Caroline Quentin head to Norway to discover four extraordinary homes in a country where architecture is influenced by incredible landscapes and the extreme climate. Properties include Vega Cottage, on Vega Island, 600km north of Oslo near the Arctic Circle, a truly remote family retreat delicately perched on rocks and overlooking the beach(n)

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland(n)

programme.title: 10 Puppies and Us
programme.desc: Emma Lowe goes in search of a special puppy, wanting one that will act as a therapy dog for her four-year-old son, who has Down's syndrome, and soothe him when he is distressed. Meanwhile, newlyweds Leigh and Bella are forced to call in behaviourist Louise Glazebrook when their new pug Butch Cassidy has trouble sleeping on a night. There are also catch-ups with salon owner Hannah, who is taking on her third Chihuahua, and psychic Delia as she welcomes the big-breed Leonberger she fell in love with. But as the small puppy grows, she finds herself completely out of her depth(n)

programme.title: Cities: Nature's New Wild
programme.desc: Animals displaced from their habitats by human expansion, including elephants in Sri Lanka and capuchin monkeys on the Pacific coast of Costa Rica. The programme also examines situations where humans have learned to live alongside their animal neighbours, including a heron that has been visiting an Amsterdam house twice a day for 17 years and a project to preserve manatees in Florida that has saved them from being an endangered species(n)

programme.title: Dad's Army
programme.desc: On a dark and stormy night, Jonesy's van runs out of petrol and the platoon is forced to spend the night in a spooky deserted house, where the men's imaginations soon run wild. Vintage comedy first aired in 1973, starring Arthur Lowe, John Le Mesurier and Clive Dunn(n)

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards one of the world's most famous trains, the Rocky Mountaineer, to cross the backbone of the North American continent from Kamloops to the spa resort of Banff. This magnificent journey takes him to the highest point of the 19th century transcontinental Canadian Pacific Railway line at Kicking Horse Pass, past Lake Louise and inside spiral tunnels blasted through the mountains. Along the way, Michael hears of the harsh and dangerous conditions endured by the Chinese and European labourers who built the railway, and looks back at the historic driving of the Last Spike, which completed the Canadian Pacific Railway in 1885(n)

programme.title: QI XL
programme.desc: In an extended version, Sandi Toksvig looks at 'Quills', inviting Jimmy Carr, Tom Allen and Lou Sanders to join regular panellist Alan Davies to face her fiendish questions. As usual, the host doesn't particularly care whether the answers are right or not, so long as they are interesting(n)

programme.title: Live at the Apollo
programme.desc: Phil Wang comperes the comedy show at London's Hammersmith Apollo. He warms up the audience before introducing routines by star of The Mash Report Rachel Parris and actor, voiceover artist and stand-up Brennan Reece(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: A desperate Andre needs help when Melody does not come home from her goodbye party, and the ensuing hunt across the entire West Side forces Franklin to confront the realities of his empire. Julia proposes a new plan to Teddy and Gustavo clears the way for his own vision. Crime drama, starring Damson Idris and Marcus Henderson(n)

programme.title: Snowfall
programme.desc: Despite being careful, Teddy and Franklin suddenly find themselves compromised and with their operation potentially crashing down around their ears. Mel is still addicted and Andre knows he will have to choose between protecting the neighbourhood or protecting her. Crime drama, starring Damson Idris and Marcus Henderson(n)

programme.title: In the Dark Half (2012)
programme.desc: A teenager babysits a six-year-old neighbour, but is distraught when he unexpectedly dies in her care. While the dead child's father looks for someone to blame, she is tormented by the belief that his spirit is haunting the abandoned bunker she has made her refuge from her troubles. Horror, starring Jessica Barden, Tony Curran and Lyndsey Marshal(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: The Instant Gardener
programme.desc: Presenter Helen Skelton and expert garden designer Danny Clarke transform a garden in Solihull to provide a special boy with a wealth of sensory stimulation(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Comedian Bobby Davro and songstress Sheila Ferguson of the Three Degrees compete to find profit-making antiques in Kent, guided by experts James Braxton and Catherine Southon. Away from the contest, Bobby hears the little-known history of the Royal Engineers, while Sheila discovers how black culture and music arrived - and thrived - in the Garden of England(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher meets Essex farmer David Eagle, who has let the sea inundate acres and acres of his farmland to create vital saltmarsh habitat for migratory birds(n)

programme.title: Beechgrove Repotted
programme.desc: Visiting gardens and people who see the garden as an artist's palette, from a topiary zoo to a woman who makes her art out of other people's rubbish. Plus, a visit to EA Hornell's garden in Kirkcudbright(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein: From Venice to Istanbul
programme.desc: The chef continues his journey through Croatia and savours the native oysters of Ston, before crossing the border into Albania, where he is joined by his son Jack and the pair sample local dishes such as chicken jufka and kokoretsi. In his own kitchen on the Greek island of Symi, Rick cooks traditional Albanian dishes, including Lepur Comlek and Tave Kosi(n)

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: In light of Britain's baking obsession, actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of recipes from television chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the final episode, Michael Mosley investigates whether simply thinking about food can help you lose weight, and Giles Yeo finds out how to keep your skin young and healthy. Meanwhile, Alain Gregoire tests whether listening to music improves your sleep, and Guddi Singh examines the latest diet in the headlines which claims to have a good impact on the planet(n)

programme.title: Painting the Holy Land
programme.desc: Lachlan Goudie explores the role of Mary in the Bible, and why she has become one of the figures in Christianity most heavily represented in art(n)

programme.title: Dunkirk (1958)
programme.desc: Soldiers are separated from their comrades-in-arms during the Dunkirk evacuation and face a terrifying journey to safety. A heroic lance corporal takes charge, aiming to lead the platoon to sanctuary while those around him panic. Second World War adventure, starring John Mills, Richard Attenborough, Robert Urquhart and Bernard Lee(n)

programme.title: Undiscovered Worlds with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: Steve Backshall searches for remains of the Mayan civilisation in the Yucatan, exploring a network of underground caves that were believed to be a supernatural world. The expedition uncovers a flooded cavern filled with the remains of human sacrifice victims, as well as artefacts that reveal a less disturbing side to this ancient culture(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Highlights
programme.desc: John Inverdale presents action from the third round of fixtures, featuring Italy v Scotland at Stadio Olimpico, Wales v France at Principality Stadium and England v Ireland at Twickenham. Italy and Scotland were aiming for their first wins of the campaign, while France and Ireland faced tough tests as they looked to maintain their Grand Slam hopes(n)

programme.title: Russia with Simon Reeve
programme.desc: On the 100th anniversary of the Russian Revolution, adventurer Simon Reeve embarks on a journey across the world's biggest country. He begins in Russia's far east, and the snow-capped volcanoes of Kamchatka and Yakutia, where reindeer herders live in temperatures as low as minus 30. Outside the eastern port of Vladivostok, Simon investigates the growing influence of China on the area, then passes through the Boreal Forest, where he meets a conservationist dedicated to protecting the Amur tiger. His final destination is a giant hole in the ground in Siberia(n)

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Paddy McGuinness, Freddie Flintoff and Chris Harris take the latest sports cars from Ferrari, Aston Martin and Porsche on a Yorkshire road trip. Back on the track, Chris tests out Volkswagen's ID-R electric racer. YouTube star KSI is the studio guest(n)

programme.title: Win the Wilderness: Alaska
programme.desc: Duane and Rena question each couple about their plans for the home to see if any of them are harbouring doubts about their ability to make a success of life in the wilderness. This round of questioning results in one couple being eliminated from the contest, while the remaining three are separated from each other for two days to test their ability to cope with isolation(n)

programme.title: The Ranganation
programme.desc: Romesh Ranganathan hosts a topical comedy show, in which a diverse group of 25 members of the public drawn from all walks of life are invited to share their thoughts on the state of modern Britain today. Romesh and his outspoken and passionate focus group will be joined in the studio by Jon Richardson(n)

programme.title: Frankie Boyle's Tour of Scotland
programme.desc: The comedian explores Glasgow, calling in at a martial arts club in the east end and exploring the ruins of Crookston Castle. Frankie's home city is no stranger to riots and protest and he meets one high-profile campaigner who became famous as one of the 'Glasgow Girls'. On the journey to his stand-up show at the Gaiety Theatre in Ayr, Frankie pops into the town of Troon to meet some members of one of the most famous showmen families in Scotland. Upon reaching Ayr, he catches up with fellow performer Elaine C Smith to discuss performing onstage and their different styles of comedy(n)

programme.title: Noah (2014)
programme.desc: A man is chosen by God to build an ark capable of housing his family and pairs of every living creature when the world is flooded. However, Noah's mission is complicated by his adopted daughter Ila, and the villainous Tubal-cain, who plans on storming the vessel to escape the Creator's vengeance. Biblical epic, starring Russell Crowe, Ray Winstone and Emma Watson(n)

programme.title: World News Today
programme.desc: The day's leading stories(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Bruno Brookes host the edition first shown on March 30, with performances by Pat and Mick, the Bangles, Roachford, Brother Beyond, Paul Simpson featuring Adeva, Transvision Vamp, the The, Guns N' Roses, Fuzzbox, the Cult, Madonna and Kon Kan(n)

programme.title: Sings Disney Songs
programme.desc: Interpretations of music from the Walt Disney Studios' films over the past 40 years, featuring songs including the Bare Necessities, When You Wish Upon a Star and Zip-a-Dee-Doo-Dah. Celine Dion and Elton John are among the artists on clips from shows including Top of the Pops, Blue Peter, Shirley Bassey, It's Lulu, Louis Armstrong's Show of the Week and Brubeck(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Anthea Turner host the edition first shown on April 6, featuring performances by Brother Beyond, Kon Kan, Holly Johnson, Paula Abdul, Coldcut with Lisa Stansfield, Simply Red, Transvision Vamp, Madonna and INXS(n)

programme.title: Rock Island Line: The Song That Made Britain Rock
programme.desc: Billy Bragg offers a salute to American folk song Rock Island Line, which emerged in the late 1920s. Its first recorded performance of was by inmates of the Arkansas Cummins State Farm prison in 1934. After several covers, Lonnie Donegan's recording, released as a single in late 1955, signalled the start of the UK skiffle craze(n)

programme.title: Chas and Dave: Last Orders
programme.desc: Documentary offering an insight into Chas Hodges and Dave Peacock's successful five decades in the music industry, which has seen them share the same bill as Led Zeppelin and make a big impact on London's cultural landscape. The film blends archive performances with more recent tour footage and features contributions by Pete Doherty, Jools Holland and Phill Jupitus. Narrated by Arthur Smith(n)

programme.title: What Ever Happened to Rock 'n' Roll?
programme.desc: Lauren Laverne leads a discussion from London's 100 Club asking whether rock 'n' roll is in crisis and what it represents in contemporary society. Can the genre still be considered as dangerous and subversive as it once was or has it become more about lifestyle and decoration? Participants include Savages' lead singer Jehnny Beth, Dr John Cooper Clarke and former Animal Eric Burdon. There are also contributions from Noel Gallagher and Dave Grohl and the members of Sleaford Mods and Alabama Shakes(n)

programme.title: Rock 'n' Roll Britannia
programme.desc: A look back at the beginnings of British rock 'n' roll, when acts such as the Shadows and Johnny Kidd and the Pirates helped to lay the foundations of an enduring musical culture. Including the current line-up of the Quarrymen - forerunners of the Beatles - performing Rock Island Line, and contributions by Cliff Richard, Marty Wilde, Joe Brown and Bruce Welch. Narrated by Roger McGough(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: Portrait of the rock musician, following her shopping for clothes in Paris, hanging out with Sandra Bernhard in New York, spending time in London and taking a special trip back to her home town of Akron, Ohio. The documentary also features Chrissie giving a live performance at one of London's newest venues(n)

programme.title: Rock Island Line: The Song That Made Britain Rock
programme.desc: Billy Bragg offers a salute to American folk song Rock Island Line, which emerged in the late 1920s. Its first recorded performance of was by inmates of the Arkansas Cummins State Farm prison in 1934. After several covers, Lonnie Donegan's recording, released as a single in late 1955, signalled the start of the UK skiffle craze(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss(n)

programme.title: South Pacific
programme.desc: A look at how the 20,000 islands in the region became colonised by humans and animals. For the Polynesians, the Pacific's first people, their journeys constituted a feat of navigational ingenuity, while animals often had to rely on violent forces of nature, such as cyclones and tsunamis, to reach the shores(n)

programme.title: Hidden
programme.desc: Connor confides in Mia about Geraint Ellis, and she convinces him that the old man deserved what happened to him. He still feels isolated after his fight with Lee, until he is reluctantly persuaded to go to a rave at the quarry. In English and Welsh(n)

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from Rio
programme.desc: Clive visits the Brazilian city of Rio de Janeiro, where the poor live by their wits in the favelas while the wealthy make contact with their less fortunate neighbours only when a servant is paid or a millionaire is mugged(n)

programme.title: The Midwife (2017)
programme.desc: Claire works as a midwife at a maternity hospital that is soon to be shut down. One day, her late father's former mistress, whom Claire resents for having abandoned him shortly before he died, decides to come back into her life. Drama, starring Catherine Deneuve, Catherine Frot and Olivier Gourmet. In French(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Bruno Brookes host the edition first shown on March 30, with performances by Pat and Mick, the Bangles, Roachford, Brother Beyond, Paul Simpson featuring Adeva, Transvision Vamp, the The, Guns N' Roses, Fuzzbox, the Cult, Madonna and Kon Kan(n)

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1989
programme.desc: Simon Mayo and Anthea Turner host the edition first shown on April 6, featuring performances by Brother Beyond, Kon Kan, Holly Johnson, Paula Abdul, Coldcut with Lisa Stansfield, Simply Red, Transvision Vamp, Madonna and INXS(n)

programme.title: Tap America: How a Nation Found Its Feet
programme.desc: Actor and writer Clarke Peters explores the origins, development and modern significance of tap dancing - the art form he has loved since he was a child. He looks at 17th-century accounts of the dances performed by African slaves on American soil, as well as dance-offs and contests between Irish and African-American dancers in the 1800s, and the troubled Hollywood heyday of tap in the 1930s and 40s, when black performers were routinely excluded from film roles(n)

programme.title: Rick Stein's Long Weekends
programme.desc: The chef visits Vienna, the city that once ran the Austro-Hungarian Empire and continues to be home to comfort dishes such as tafelspitz and goulash, and which gave its name to one of Europe's most popular dishes - the Wiener schnitzel. Rick learns the essential etiquette to the Austrian capital's coffee house culture and indulges in its sweet tooth by enjoying a plate or two of apple strudel and Sacher torte. He also finds time to enjoy the traditional music of the country, typically Mozart and Strauss(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: Iolo's Snowdonia
programme.desc: As winter arrives, Iolo endures life-threatening wind chill, ice and snow on the Carneddau high peaks. A cold snap takes him to one of his old roaming grounds on the Berwyn moors, where he finds a solitary robin surviving in thick snow. Opposite Portmeirion, the Dwyryd estuary is teeming with birds feeding. Iolo also heads to Ceunant Llennyrch, a gorge hidden by an ancient woodland that has existed since trees first colonised Snowdonia after the last ice age(n)

programme.title: Attenborough: 60 Years in the Wild
programme.desc: David Attenborough reflects on environmental changes during his film-making career, the pioneering conservationists whose footsteps he followed and the revolution in attitudes toward nature. On a tropical beach in Malaysia, David recalls how in the 1950s he dug up turtle eggs to eat and releases newly hatched turtles into the wild. Finally he asks whether changing attitudes and greater knowledge of the world will be enough to save it for future generations(n)

programme.title: Art on the BBC
programme.desc: Art historian Rose Balston explores six decades of BBC archive to discover how TV has influenced our understanding of John Constable. The artist pushed the boundaries of landscape painting, to the extent he was rejected by the establishment, but, perhaps just as surprisingly, he poured his love for one woman on to the canvas as no painter of landscapes had done before(n)

programme.title: Britain's Lost Masterpieces
programme.desc: Art experts Jacky Klein and Dr Bendor Grosvenor visit Haddo House in Aberdeenshire, which may be the home to some of Scotland's greatest lost paintings. They also head to the storerooms of Montrose Museum, where a portrait of Richard Mead purported to be a copy may actually be the original(n)

programme.title: Britain's Outlaws: Highwaymen, Pirates and Rogues
programme.desc: Historian Sam Willis explores the criminal underworld of Britain in the 17th and 18th centuries, beginning with the arrival of the highwayman in the wake of the English Civil War. Heavily romanticised in literature, these glamorous gangsters became a social menace on the roads and a political thorn in the side of the creaking British state, but a far darker reality lay underneath the dashing image of stylish robbers on horseback(n)

And in Jordan, Hamza, who's eight, has a day out in town with his dad, and buys a lovely present for his baby sister, Mimi. programme.title: Six Nations Sin Bin
programme.desc: Gabby Logan and Gareth Thomas are joined by guests as they review the most recent Six Nations matches and look ahead to upcoming fixtures including Wales' clash with France. Plus, Wales, England, Ireland and Scotland battle it out to see who can be crowned the Sin Bin Showdown champion(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City, plus updates on the lunchtime kick-off between Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v France (Kick-off 4.45pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of both teams' third match of the championship, which takes place at Principality Stadium in Cardiff. Wales won 24-19 in Paris last year and went on to win the Grand Slam. However they were defeated by Ireland last time out to ensure they cannot repeat the feat this year, while France have won their opening two contests against England and Italy. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede and Martin Johnson, pitchside reports by Sonja McLaughlan and Paul O'Connell, and commentary from Eddie Butler and Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special 1970s edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Valerie Leon, Mick Miller, Steve Harley, Mike Batt, John H Stracey, Tina Charles, Zandra Rhodes and Britt Ekland(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Violette tries her best to get clean in rehab, and is prepared to fight Ruby for custody of baby Harmony. However, a bad decision leads to devastating consequences for the sisters. Faith struggles to cope with Luka's diagnosis and considers joining a support group for parents of children with cancer, and turns to Dylan for comfort. After complaining to Will about his latest dating rejection, Ethan meets Fenisha at the vending machine. Starring Kelly Gough, Maddy Hill, Kirsty Mitchell and Jack Nolan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge and Leicester City v Manchester City at King Power Stadium. The clash between London rivals Chelsea and Spurs could have a major bearing on who finishes in the top four, while Leicester and the Citizens look to be competing for second place. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Southampton v Aston Villa(n)

programme.title: At Any Price (2012)
programme.desc: A farmer strives to grow his family business by any means necessary, but his rebellious youngest son proves a constant disappointment, more interested in pursuing a career as a racing driver than helping with the farm. When a series of crises threaten the family's future, father and son must learn to put aside their differences to avert the disaster. Drama, starring Dennis Quaid and Zac Efron(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Northumbria University's Students' Union in Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Developers Lucy Alexander and Martin Roberts explore properties about to be auctioned, then follow the sale and talk to the buyers about their plans for the buildings. After three months the team returns to review their progress(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help teams to scour an antiques fair at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings(n)

programme.title: Tudur's TV Flashback
programme.desc: Host Tudur Owen discovers the truth about family life, referencing the funniest, most fascinating clips from half a century and more of Welsh television. It's a story of disastrous holidays, arranged marriages and wonky houses, full of lively characters from the nation's TV past(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Pershore Abbey in Worcestershire to find out how the community is preparing for Lent. James Lusted meets an octogenarian who cooks for those in need every week(n)

programme.title: Lifeline
programme.desc: An appeal on behalf of the charity StreetGames, which harnesses the power of sport to create positive change in the lives of disadvantaged young people across the UK.(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon on a house-hunting mission with a professional singer, who has a £670,000 budget to buy a country home. Nicki also heads to Dartmoor National Park(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team transforms a garden in Shoreham-by-Sea into the perfect place for John and David to host their upcoming wedding reception(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steven Fletcher and Kirsten Ramsay restore a 19th-century clock, George Blackman rebuilds a broken typewriter and Suzie Fletcher repairs a Second World War pilot's bomber jacket(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Great CyberWar, the Doctor arrives in the far future, intent on protecting the last of the human race from the deadly Cybermen. But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Watford at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Norwich City at Molineux and Arsenal v Everton at Emirates Stadium. The stakes were high for all six teams in action, with Watford and Norwich fighting for survival and the other sides all hoping to play European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals in the top flight(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather. Presented by Jon Kay and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Animal Park Summer Special
programme.desc: The penguin keepers embark on their most ambitious project to date, building an underwater feeding machine, while a hyrax is rushed to the vet for some emergency dental work. Jean Johansson discovers how wolves like to bury their food so they can come back to eat it when they are hungry. Ben Fogle and Kate Humble meet a new creature at the park - and it is the male, not the female that gives birth to their young(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the development progress of properties in Canterbury in Kent, London's Crystal Palace and the Cumbria village of Branthwaite(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under Revisited
programme.desc: Jayne Myburgh thought her son Albie's rare genetic disorder would be greater controlled in the warmth of the Australian sun. Her South African-born husband Eugene was well aware of his wife's dreams, but needed more than a promise of sunshine to convince him he should move to the other side of the world. Nicki Chapman finds out whether or not they made the life-changing decision to relocate Down Under(n)

programme.title: Defenders UK
programme.desc: Enforcement officers investigate the death of a car wash worker, and catch two crooks dealing in counterfeit medicines worth £5million(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Charlie Ross hosts from the Agricultural Showground on Anglesey, where antique experts Roo Irvine and Richard Madley guide two teams as they search for items of interest and value to take to auction. Away from the contest, Charlie also meets a familiar face with a passion for Georgian glass(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara goes into battle for Daniel's job at the prison and Emma is surprised by the strength of her feelings for Jasmine. Tragedy strikes for Al and a homeless mother, while Karen and Rob start a new fostering chapter(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives investigating criminal acts in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a budget of £300,00 find a country property that gives them community on their doorstep and a crash pad for their grandchildren to stay during the holidays. Margherita also meets a Devon stovemaker and finds out the practical and environmental benefits of this popular country home interior feature(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three influential farm workers - all critical to the success of their farms - go head to head, hoping to be named Farm Worker of the Year at the prestigious Farmers Weekly Awards(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson reach the final leg of their trip and steer their classic car through Yorkshire. Charles decides to spend as much of his kitty as possible, hoping a bigger outlay will result in huge profits. He goes for age, finding a pair of expensive 17th-century oak chairs. Margie also goes 17th century, but finds a crib that's much more within budget. The final auction is in Harrogate, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Reporting Scotland; Weather
programme.desc: Presented by Sally Magnusson(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rob Beckett present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace left-back Patrick van Aanholt, former Premier League star Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink, England cricketer Samit Patel and tennis player Naomi Broady join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos and panic ensues as the party goers struggle to comprehend what has happened. As the grave situation grows worse, multiple lives are left hanging in the balance and one person is set to meet a tragic end(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: Bumps
programme.desc: Sixty-two-year-old divorcee Anita is concerned that despite having two grown up kids, there is no stork on the horizon to bring her grandchildren. Her daughter Joanne cannot have children and her son Aiden has zero interest. After hearing about an Indian woman in her seventies giving birth, Anita secretly undergoes treatment - only to learn Joanne has also managed to conceive and is due at the same time. One-off comedy, starring Amanda Redman, with Lisa McGrillis and Seb Cardinal(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Zara goes into battle for Daniel's job at the prison and Emma is surprised by the strength of her feelings for Jasmine. Tragedy strikes for Al and a homeless mother, while Karen and Rob start a new fostering chapter(n)

programme.title: Shakespeare & Hathaway - Private Investigators
programme.desc: Frank and Lu must track down a vital witness in a crown court trial. Mark Benton and Jo Joyner star in the comedy drama about an oddball couple of private detectives investigating criminal acts in Stratford-upon-Avon(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Margherita Taylor helps a couple with a budget of £300,00 find a country property that gives them community on their doorstep and a crash pad for their grandchildren to stay during the holidays. Margherita also meets a Devon stovemaker and finds out the practical and environmental benefits of this popular country home interior feature(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Three influential farm workers - all critical to the success of their farms - go head to head, hoping to be named Farm Worker of the Year at the prestigious Farmers Weekly Awards(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Margie Cooper and Charles Hanson reach the final leg of their trip and steer their classic car through Yorkshire. Charles decides to spend as much of his kitty as possible, hoping a bigger outlay will result in huge profits. He goes for age, finding a pair of expensive 17th-century oak chairs. Margie also goes 17th century, but finds a crib that's much more within budget. The final auction is in Harrogate, North Yorkshire(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Rob Beckett present the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design a garden for a family of seven in Stamford, Lincolnshire, who have just moved from rural Rutland and miss the countryside. With five noisy children, the parents want a peaceful garden that they can call their own, but the awkward plot gives the presenters a huge challenge, despite a budget of £4,500(n)

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: Crystal Palace left-back Patrick van Aanholt, former Premier League star Jimmy Floyd Hasselbaink, England cricketer Samit Patel and tennis player Naomi Broady join team captains Matt Dawson and Phil Tufnell and host Sue Barker on the quiz(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Chaos and panic ensues as the party goers struggle to comprehend what has happened. As the grave situation grows worse, multiple lives are left hanging in the balance and one person is set to meet a tragic end(n)

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Host Rob Brydon and team captains Lee Mack and David Mitchell return for another episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show. Among the participants setting out to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible fibs about themselves are Clare Balding, Liz Bonnin, Jo Brand, Asim Chaudhry, Victoria Coren Mitchell, Greg Davies, Simon Day, Greg James, Guz Khan, Gabby Logan, Chris McCausland, Stephen Merchant, Kiri Pritchard-McLean, Angela Scanlon, John Simm, Ranj Singh, Henning Wehn, Claudia Winkleman and Lucy Worsley(n)

programme.title: Bumps
programme.desc: Sixty-two-year-old divorcee Anita is concerned that despite having two grown up kids, there is no stork on the horizon to bring her grandchildren. Her daughter Joanne cannot have children and her son Aiden has zero interest. After hearing about an Indian woman in her seventies giving birth, Anita secretly undergoes treatment - only to learn Joanne has also managed to conceive and is due at the same time. One-off comedy, starring Amanda Redman, with Lisa McGrillis and Seb Cardinal(n)

programme.title: Soft Border Patrol
programme.desc: With equal marriage legal in Northern Ireland, Ben and Kris encounter equal rights on the border when they meet divorce lawyer Hector. The continuity Nine gang returns with new plans to unite Ulster by harnessing water power, while TV host Gerry Kelly has a bone to pick with Laurence Lyle. Michael Condron, Chris Patrick-Simpson, Karen Hassan and Neil Delamere star(n)

programme.title: The Graham Norton Show
programme.desc: Graham brings the 26th series of his hit chat show to an end by dipping back into its archives to recall some of the more memorable verbal exchanges. Stars appearing include Robert De Niro, Bruce Springsteen, Arnold Schwarzenegger, Nadiya Hussain, Emilia Clarke, Regina King, Julie Andrews, Jennifer Aniston, Reese Witherspoon, Olivia Colman, Helena Bonham Carter, Elizabeth Banks, Ricky Gervais, Lewis Hamilton, Hillary Clinton, Dwayne Johnson, Kevin Hart, Jodie Whittaker, Judi Dench, Jennifer Hudson, Hugh Grant, Daisy Ridley, Ruth Jones, Motsi Mabuse, Anthony Joshua and Tom Hanks(n)

programme.title: RuPaul's Drag Race UK
programme.desc: Tensions are high and emotions are charged as the remaining four queens are challenged to give family members a very special drag makeover. Bafta winner Michaela Coel joins Michelle Visage and Alan Carr on the judging panel as another queen is eliminated from the competition(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: National and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45. Presented by Jon Kay and Rachel Burden(n)

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt is joined by chefs Jordan Bourke and Anna Haugh, and special guest Robert Bathurst faces his food heaven or hell(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In the final episode, the chef and the group of volunteers discover how much weight they have lost over the past two months, and how much fitter they are as a result of all their hard work. To celebrate their achievements, Tom decides to throw a special picnic by the river, and whips up a series of exciting, healthy recipes they can all enjoy(n)

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests look ahead to the weekend's fixtures, including Leicester City v Manchester City, plus updates on the lunchtime kick-off between Chelsea and Tottenham Hotspur(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Anita Manning hosts from the British Motor Museum in Warwickshire, where Richard Madley and Philip Serrell help two teams select items to take to auction(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The return of the makeover show, with Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich designing gardens for a man in Southampton, who has a £5,000 budget for a party space to entertain his friends, but which must also have a feeling of his Welsh homeland about it(n)

programme.title: Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Claudia Winkleman presents the final, which sees the last three contestants rising to the challenge. To shake things up, however, the judges present their tasks in a different order to normal. Chris Bavin's 'rustle up' comes first, after which it's on to another of Angela Hartnett's tricky recipes in the eliminator. Sadly, one of the hopefuls will then receive their marching orders, leaving the other two to take on Mary Berry's challenge - a full three-course meal which will secure one of them the title(n)

programme.title: Live Six Nations Rugby Union
programme.desc: Wales v France (Kick-off 4.45pm). Gabby Logan presents coverage of both teams' third match of the championship, which takes place at Principality Stadium in Cardiff. Wales won 24-19 in Paris last year and went on to win the Grand Slam. However they were defeated by Ireland last time out to ensure they cannot repeat the feat this year, while France have won their opening two contests against England and Italy. With analysis from Thomas Castaignede and Martin Johnson, pitchside reports by Sonja McLaughlan and Paul O'Connell, and commentary from Eddie Butler and Jonathan Davies(n)

programme.title: The Greatest Dancer
programme.desc: Alesha Dixon and Jordan Banjo host the dancing contest, featuring performers from a variety of styles, including ballet, jazz, street, ballroom, contemporary and disco. Tonight, the competition continues as the remaining seven acts take to the stage, competing for a chance to showcase their skills on Strictly Come Dancing and take home the £50,000 prize(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present a special 1970s edition of the general knowledge quiz, featuring Valerie Leon, Mick Miller, Steve Harley, Mike Batt, John H Stracey, Tina Charles, Zandra Rhodes and Britt Ekland(n)

programme.title: Casualty
programme.desc: Violette tries her best to get clean in rehab, and is prepared to fight Ruby for custody of baby Harmony. However, a bad decision leads to devastating consequences for the sisters. Faith struggles to cope with Luka's diagnosis and considers joining a support group for parents of children with cancer, and turns to Dylan for comfort. After complaining to Will about his latest dating rejection, Ethan meets Fenisha at the vending machine. Starring Kelly Gough, Maddy Hill, Kirsty Mitchell and Jack Nolan(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge and Leicester City v Manchester City at King Power Stadium. The clash between London rivals Chelsea and Spurs could have a major bearing on who finishes in the top four, while Leicester and the Citizens look to be competing for second place. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Southampton v Aston Villa(n)

programme.title: At Any Price (2012)
programme.desc: A farmer strives to grow his family business by any means necessary, but his rebellious youngest son proves a constant disappointment, more interested in pursuing a career as a racing driver than helping with the farm. When a series of crises threaten the family's future, father and son must learn to put aside their differences to avert the disaster. Drama, starring Dennis Quaid and Zac Efron(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: A round-up of national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Presented by Chris Mason(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Chelsea v Tottenham Hotspur at Stamford Bridge and Leicester City v Manchester City at King Power Stadium. The clash between London rivals Chelsea and Spurs could have a major bearing on who finishes in the top four, while Leicester and the Citizens look to be competing for second place. Plus, Burnley v Bournemouth, Crystal Palace v Newcastle United, Sheffield United v Brighton & Hove Albion and Southampton v Aston Villa(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00(n)

programme.title: The Big Questions
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents moral and religious discussions on topical issues, with contributions by members of the audience at Northumbria University's Students' Union in Newcastle(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts and Dion Dublin follow the progress of a house development in Knutton, Staffordshire, a one-bedroom flat in London's Cricklewood and an ambitious project in Belper in Derbyshire(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Caroline Hawley and Raj Bisram help teams to scour an antiques fair at the Royal Welsh Showground in Builth Wells, with Eric Knowles overseeing proceedings(n)

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher visits Pershore Abbey in Worcestershire to find out how the community is preparing for Lent. James Lusted meets an octogenarian who cooks for those in need every week(n)

programme.title: Community Life
programme.desc: This edition features Luminous Soul, a dance initiative for people with disabilities, and also includes information about volunteering opportunities with DeafblindNI(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman is in Devon on a house-hunting mission with a professional singer, who has a £670,000 budget to buy a country home. Nicki also heads to Dartmoor National Park(n)

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The team transforms a garden in Shoreham-by-Sea into the perfect place for John and David to host their upcoming wedding reception(n)

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Steven Fletcher and Kirsten Ramsay restore a 19th-century clock, George Blackman rebuilds a broken typewriter and Suzie Fletcher repairs a Second World War pilot's bomber jacket(n)

programme.title: Spy in the Wild
programme.desc: In the final episode of the series, the spy cameras travel to the Arctic and Antarctic circles, following chicks in a king penguin colony on the island of South Georgia in the Southern Ocean. On the other side of the world, Spy Walrus discovers a walrus family hauled out on an ice floe, before Spy Gyrfalcon follows the family as they head towards dry land. Elsewhere on Norway's Hornoya island, Spy Puffin follows thousands of male puffins as they gather in the snow and search for their life-long partners(n)

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker, Ellie Harrison and Steve Brown visit Shaftesbury in Dorset to take part in the town's annual snowdrop festival. Plus, Adam Henson meets a trailblazing vet(n)

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: In the aftermath of the Great CyberWar, the Doctor arrives in the far future, intent on protecting the last of the human race from the deadly Cybermen. But in the face of such a relentless enemy, has she put her best friends at risk? What terrors lie hiding in the depths of space, and what is Ko Sharmus? Guest starring Julie Graham, Patrick O'Kane and Steve Toussaint(n)

programme.title: Call the Midwife
programme.desc: Sister Julienne decides it is time to take matters in hand when a letter arrives from the council saying that it will cut funding to Nonnatus House and no longer pay for the accommodation, and goes to see Violet at one of her open sessions as a local councillor. At clinic, Nurse Crane meets and cares for heavily pregnant Bonnie, who is not married but committed to the baby's father Eddie, while Kevin's dependency on pain relief is discovered by the team and Dr Turner and Miss Higgins must find a way to help him(n)

programme.title: Last Tango in Halifax
programme.desc: Alan and Celia are not seeing eye to eye seven years into their marriage, while tempers fray at Caroline and Gillian's joint birthday when Celia can't hold her tongue. Return of Sally Wainwright's drama, starring Derek Jacobi, Anne Reid, Sarah Lancashire, Nicola Walker, Tony Gardner and Dean Andrews(n)

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman presents highlights of the latest Premier League fixtures - Manchester United v Watford at Old Trafford, Wolverhampton Wanderers v Norwich City at Molineux and Arsenal v Everton at Emirates Stadium. The stakes were high for all six teams in action, with Watford and Norwich fighting for survival and the other sides all hoping to play European football next season. Plus, a round-up of yesterday's goals in the top flight(n)

programme.title: The Women's Football Show
programme.desc: Reshmin Chowdhury presents highlights of the weekend's games in the Super League, including Arsenal v Reading at Meadow Park and Manchester City v Chelsea at Academy Stadium, as the title race between the Gunners, City and the Blues continued(n)

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detectives investigate two murders in which the victims have been placed in water and surrounded by flowers. Vera and sidekick Joe believe the culprit may be linked to a group of bird-watching friends, but despite their increasingly tangled and dysfunctional relationships, the twitchers seem determined to protect one another. The very first episode of the drama adapted from Ann Cleeves' novel Hidden Depths, starring Brenda Blethyn, David Leon, Gina McKee, Wunmi Mosaku, Paul Ritter and Neil Armstrong(n)

programme.title: England Rugby: Rising Sons
programme.desc: In 2019, members of the England rugby union squad faced the biggest challenge of their careers - to win the Rugby World Cup in the Far East after disappointing on home soil in the same competition four years earlier. This documentary records the inside story, with cameras capturing the squad's journey and exploits in Japan, offering exclusive behind-the-scenes access as the tournament unfolded and the team went in search of glory. Featuring interviews with head coach Eddie Jones and captain Owen Farrell(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Henry Firth and Ian Theasby serve up their version of classic shepherd's pie, a simple recipe for fudgy chocolate cookies, sushi with two different fillings and a bakewell tart. Their guest is chef and baker Sal Dhalla, who cooks her roasted vegetable farinata(n)

programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Over the past 25 years, portion sizes have increased by 100 per cent - and plates have got bigger too - none of which is good news for people's diet. With this in mind, Ranj Singh checks out the breakfast buffet to see whether people know how many calories they are piling on their plates and Sian Williams tests off-the-shelf portion-control gadgets, including smaller cutlery to help eat more slowly. Plus, two more diets are put to the test - the Metabolism Reset Diet, based around a detox for the liver, and Bosh! Healthy Vegan, which means ditching the junk(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Jill Douglas presents coverage of the opening game in the third round of fixtures, which takes place at the Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Both sides are without a win so far, with Italy showing some improvement against France after a heavy defeat to Wales, and Scotland producing spirited displays in narrow losses to Ireland and England. With analysis from Ian McGeechan, Maggie Alphonsi, Gareth Thomas and Marco Bortolami, and commentary by Miles Harrison, Scott Hastings and Ben Kay(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Compilation of some of the most dramatic endgames from the arcade-themed quiz show hosted by Ben Shephard, in which the last contestant standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Despicable Me 3 (2017)
programme.desc: Dismissed from the Anti-Villain League after botching a mission, Gru sets off to meet his long-lost twin brother. Animated sequel, with the voices of Steve Carell, Kristen Wiig and Trey Parker(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo return with a new series of the entertainment show, in which Ant v Dec goes global. This series will feature the biggest ever giveaway in British TV history and a finale coming from a very special location. In the first episode, Stephen Mulhern joins Ant and Dec in the studio(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Emma Willis hosts the first of this year's Battle Rounds in the singing contest. Coaches will.i.am, Sir Tom Jones, Olly Murs and Meghan Trainor all return to their infamous red chairs as the acts they have chosen from the blind auditions go head to head and fight for their place on their coach's team. The singers that impress their coach the most remain in the competition with a chance of winning a recording contract. Will those that aren't so lucky be stolen by another coach or will they be going home?(n)

programme.title: Live Boxing
programme.desc: Brad Foster v Lucien Reid. Coverage of the rematch for the British and Commonwealth Super Bantamweight titles, which takes place at the York Hall in London's Bethnal Green. The fighters shared the spoils in a majority draw when they met here five months ago, with one judge giving Reid the nod and the other two scoring it even. This time around both men will be determined to maintain their unbeaten record and ensure there is a decisive outcome in their favour(n)

programme.title: Tenable

programme.title: Ainsley's Caribbean Kitchen
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott takes a culinary tour of St Lucia, famous for its chocolate, which he puts to good use while preparing chicken bois bande with white chocolate mash potato and chocolate drop scones with chocolate banana mash up. He takes a boat trip around the island, gets a lesson in growing cocoa plants, and attends a fish fry, where he whips up some mango coconut daquiris(n)

programme.title: Living on the Veg
programme.desc: Vegan food ideas, presented by Henry Firth and Ian Theasby(n)

programme.title: The Voice UK
programme.desc: Spinning-chair singing contest, in which celebrity 'coaches' seek out the nation's best vocal talent. But there is a simple twist - with the four coaches selecting their teams through 'blind auditions', looks play no part in the selection process, meaning each singer is chosen on vocal ability alone(n)

programme.title: Ant & Dec's Saturday Night Takeaway
programme.desc: The popular duo return with a new series of the entertainment show, in which Ant v Dec goes global. This series will feature the biggest ever giveaway in British TV history and a finale coming from a very special location. In the first episode, Stephen Mulhern joins Ant and Dec in the studio(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: England v Ireland (Kick-off 3.00pm). Mark Pougatch presents all the action from the final match in the third round of fixtures, which takes place at Twickenham. England bounced back from a defeat at the hands of France by recording a 13-6 victory over Scotland at Murrayfield. However, they now face another tough test against the Irish, who are chasing the Triple Crown after wins against the Scots and Wales under new coach Andy Farrell. With analysis from Clive Woodward, Jonny Wilkinson and Brian O'Driscoll, pitchside reports by Martin Bayfield, and commentary from Nick Mullins, Lawrence Dallaglio and Gordon D'Arcy(n)

programme.title: Dancing on Ice
programme.desc: It's Movie Week as the remaining celebrities and their partners battle it out to stay in the competition and secure a place in the semi-final. Jayne Torvill and Christopher Dean head up the ice panel, alongside Ashley Banjo and John Barrowman, and there's a performance from Luke Evans alongside the professional skaters. Hosted by Phillip Schofield and Holly Willoughby(n)

programme.title: Endeavour
programme.desc: When the detective is called to investigate what appears to be a freak accident at Lady Matilda's College, he uncovers a potential link between a series of peculiar incidents across Oxford. Despite Thursday's scepticism, he becomes convinced the accidents are the result of foul play. Lady Matilda's meanwhile, is in the midst of a referendum campaign which will decide whether or not the college should become co-educational. When a woman is attacked while walking alone on a towpath, Endeavour and Thursday try to find the assailant before they strike again. Drama, starring Shaun Evans(n)

programme.title: Cilla: The Lost Tapes
programme.desc: Following her death, Cilla Black's family found a stash of films and behind-the-scenes footage from her personal video collection, along with hours of audio recording of Cilla telling her life story. Narrated by Sheridan Smith, this documentary includes interviews with the family and friends who knew Cilla best, as they watch the lost footage for the first time and recall their memories of a TV icon(n)

programme.title: Harry Redknapp's Sandbanks Summer
programme.desc: Former football manager Harry Redknapp welcomes celebrity friends to his house in Sandbanks, Britain's most exclusive and expensive seaside resort, offering an insight into this unique part of the British south coast peninsula. In the first episode, Harry is joined by Neil ‘Razor' Ruddock and Joe Pasquale. Neil is considering a move to the area, so Harry takes him and Joe to see a very desirable £5 million penthouse with some of the best views Sandbanks has to offer(n)

programme.title: The BRIT Awards 2020
programme.desc: Jack Whitehall hosts the 40th edition of the annual music awards. Stormzy, Michael Kiwanuka, Lewis Capaldi, Dave and Harry Styles are in the running for Album of the Year, while Celeste has been announced as the recipient of this year's Rising Star Award. The show will feature performances from Mabel, Harry Styles, Stormzy, Billie Eilish, Lewis Capaldi and Celeste(n)
