programme.title: Save Money: Lose Weight
programme.desc: Over the past 25 years, portion sizes have increased by 100 per cent - and plates have got bigger too - none of which is good news for people's diet. With this in mind, Ranj Singh checks out the breakfast buffet to see whether people know how many calories they are piling on their plates and Sian Williams tests off-the-shelf portion-control gadgets, including smaller cutlery to help eat more slowly. Plus, two more diets are put to the test - the Metabolism Reset Diet, based around a detox for the liver, and Bosh! Healthy Vegan, which means ditching the junk(n)

programme.title: Six Nations Live
programme.desc: Italy v Scotland (Kick-off 2.15pm). Jill Douglas presents coverage of the opening game in the third round of fixtures, which takes place at the Stadio Olimpico in Rome. Both sides are without a win so far, with Italy showing some improvement against France after a heavy defeat to Wales, and Scotland producing spirited displays in narrow losses to Ireland and England. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Penhale decides to have a Police Open day to raise community awareness, but the only genuine visitor is an angry farmer who has had three of her sheep stolen. Meanwhile, Morwenna decides to take Al away for the weekend to try out Hannah's new glamping site, but the trip does not go well as Al accidently burns down the yurt. Elsewhere, James has started biting not only Louisa but other children at the nursery. Louisa speaks to Tina about it, but is surprised to find out Tina has spoken to their lecturer about it too(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on members of the immigrant workforce after a suitcase containing the body of farm owner Darren Bailey is pulled from the river. Bill discovers that the farm had been used to launder criminal proceeds through the clever accounting of the victim's much younger Slovenian girlfriend, Lubica, who has been made sole owner of the farm's assets. This makes her a key suspect, particularly after it's revealed that her and Darren had an argument on the night he went missing. However, with many of Darren's employees seeming happy at the news of his demise, and stories coming to light about how he squeezed them at every opportunity, it isn't an open and shut case. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: The discovery of a pyre of bodies puts Bill on the hunt for Frank McGill, a hitman notorious for his weapon of choice - a nail-gun. However, having been put into witness protection years earlier after turning in other members of the Maguire gang he worked for, Frank is now off-grid, so Bill sets his sights on tracking him down. Meanwhile, DS Blair is released on bail after information is leaked regarding the unlawful obtaining of key DNA, Commissioner Metcalfe discusses the financial benefits of selling off a disused training facility and the pressure mounts for Bill to resign. Starring Rob Lowe and Rachael Stirling(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

programme.desc: An unusual message appears in Chipping Cleghorn's local newspaper, revealing details of a forthcoming murder and, while at first glance it looks like a practical joke, the information proves to be deadly accurate. Miss Marple helps Inspector Craddock with his investigation and the pair soon uncover several likely suspects in the village. Feature-length drama, starring Geraldine McEwan, with Zoe Wanamaker, Elaine Paige, Virginia McKenna, Cherie Lunghi and Frances Barber(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi seeks an alibi when Ray reports her to the police. James finds the courage to tell Ed that he is gay. Chesney and Gemma deal with an upsetting diagnosis(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate the murder of a prize-winning author on the eve of the Midsomer Literary Festival. The event goes ahead as planned, but a killing occurs during a blackout at a packed reception hosted by the widow of another writer, and it is not long before the detectives uncover corruption, sexual scandal and financial mismanagement. Guest starring Stella Gonet, with John Nettles and John Hopkins(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A barman from rural inn the Maid in Splendour is shot dead as he walks near a ruined cottage in woodlands - and Barnaby's investigation reveals a clash between charming landlord Michael Bannerman, the unofficial squire of Midsomer Worthy, and his abrasive son Stephen, who now runs the pub. John Nettles and John Hopkins star, with guest appearances by William Gaunt, Alan Cox, Dave Hill and Frances Tomelty(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby and Scott investigate crime and mayhem in the countryside as they take on a murder case in picturesque Fletcher's Cross. Undertaker Patrick Pennyman is found dead in his funeral parlour and the detectives suspect a possible link to the local spiritualist church. When another body turns up, the pressure to find the culprit redoubles. John Nettles, John Hopkins and Jane Wymark star(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Christmas special of the detective drama from 2016. The village of Little Auburn has stood abandoned since the Second World War, but now plans are afoot to restore it to its former glory via three competing projects. It is left to local landowner Roderick Craven to determine which scheme will win out, but on the eve of his big decision, one of the project leaders is murdered. DCI Barnaby and his new partner DS Jamie Winter are soon on the case, and when death stalks the streets of Little Auburn again, Barnaby and Winter are dragged into a web of conspiracy involving fraud, deadly snakes, and Roderick's estranged brother, Milo (guest star Hugh Dennis). Neil Dudgeon stars, with Nick Hendrix making his debut as Barnaby's latest assistant(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Angus, a member of the Bleakridge Watch, a group of villagers who walk the streets reporting anyone who falls foul of the law, is found dead in his meat freezer. Barnaby and Winter meet Angus's devastated sister and her daughter and learn that Angus was investigating a recent spate of burglaries in the village, but had yet to identify the culprit. Angus' post-mortem reveals strange restraining injuries on his wrists and the plot thickens as Barnaby discovers just how far some people will go for power. Frances Barber guest stars as the leader of the Watch(n)

programme.title: Foyle's War
programme.desc: It is July 1945 and, as Britain settles into peacetime, Foyle looks forward to retirement, but finds his life in turmoil once more when Samantha's employer Sir Leonard Spencer-Jones is found dead. Suspicion falls on Niko, a Russian PoW, who flees the Sussex coast and heads for a sanctuary in London - closely followed by Foyle, who has been sent to track him down by the War Office. But the investigation soon brings to light a conspiracy and an international cover-up which, if exposed, could bring down the British government. Max Brown joins the cast, with Michael Kitchen, Honeysuckle Weeks, Anthony Howell, Eleanor Bron and Christopher Good(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Long Lost Family
programme.desc: After finding out at the age of 19 that the man who raised him was not his real dad, former British soldier David Smith yearns to find the biological father he has never met. After years of dreaming, he hopes to finally know what it feels like to be called 'son'. Meanwhile, Mandy Hearn, who was adopted as a baby in 1968, hopes to trace her birth mother. However, at just 13 years old when she gave birth, she is the youngest biological mother the programme has ever encountered. Presented by Davina McCall and Nicky Campbell(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A local woman is found murdered following a row with her husband at a residents' association Spanish-themed evening. The finger of suspicion soon points to an odd-job man with a violent history, and the chairman of the association reveals he heard a squeaking noise after the victim left - but the witness promptly winds up dead himself. Crime drama, starring John Nettles(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. While out walking, Joyce spots Heldman arguing with a woman, and hours later he winds up stabbed to death with an old weapon that bears a striking resemblance to the one found during the dig. John Nettles and John Hopkins star(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: On doctor's orders, the dapper Belgian sleuth visits Brighton for what he hopes will be a relaxing holiday, but it leads to one of the most absorbing cases he has ever faced - the disappearance of a cache of jewels. Mystery, starring David Suchet, with Hermione Norris, Philip Jackson and Sorcha Cusack(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Coma victim Harriet Farmer finally wakes up a year after she was attacked while trying to elope, but her boyfriend Finn Robson is still missing. Soon after she regains consciousness, members of a chess club are targeted by a killer. Could the assault that left Harriet for dead be linked to moves on a games board? Guest starring Fay Ripley (Cold Feet), with Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Captain Perrin throws a huge party to celebrate the ship's first birthday, with executive chef Shaun creating a giant cake that is so heavy it takes 10 men to carry - and they soon come unstuck when they cannot fit the ship-shaped sponge into the lift. Passengers Norman and Maria are looking forward to attending the lavish formal party, although they will be going nowhere unless front-desk supervisor Herbert can locate their missing luggage, which contains his tuxedo and her party frock. Dancer Dulcie faces her staff appraisal, and a visit from her boyfriend helps with her homesickness(n)

programme.title: The Cruise
programme.desc: Romance is in the air for casino host Cecilia and assistant maitre d' Alberto - and she already has clear ideas about the size of her engagement ring. Things are not so happy for waitress Valeria, whose relationship ended when her boyfriend left the ship and now a close friend is leaving too. However, it isn't long until a new beau catches her eye. Timothy came on board after splitting from his partner of four years - but he has little time to worry about his broken heart in his new job with the shore excursions team, especially when he runs into hot water with a couple of misplaced passengers(n)

programme.title: Joanna Lumley's India
programme.desc: Joanna's adventures across India conclude as she explores Rajasthan, Delhi and the mountainous North West, before ending in Kashmir, the place of her birth. Her first stop takes her to the Ranthambhore National Park, where she learns about the Wildlife Protection Society of India's tiger conservation programmes. Joanna's next stop takes her to the city of Delhi, where she begins her exploration of the capital at Humayun's Tomb, before ending her 5000 mile journey in Srinagar in Kashmi, the place where she was born(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The clinic is pushed to its limits when a building collapses in Barco and the doctors face a desperate struggle to save the injured survivors. Ruby's objectivity is put to the test when she discovers a secret concerning one of her patients, and Ram helps a woman whose husband is missing(n)

programme.title: The Good Karma Hospital
programme.desc: The Royle Family's Sue Johnston guest stars in the final episode of the current series, which sees the staff of the hospital coming together to celebrate Ram and Mala's wedding. Ruby and Gabriel intervene in a dispute that has erupted between two fishermen, while a heart to heart at the wedding ceremony brings the two doctors closer together. Meanwhile, Lydia is confronted with a difficult dilemma when she receives a visit from her former mentor, who has come to the hospital seeking her help(n)

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Ken asks Samir not to push Deirdre into marriage, and Emily tells her she cannot witness the wedding as it is a sham and a lie. Meanwhile, Steve returns from his holiday in Florida(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Christine tells Steve she does not believe he has found gainful employment. Curly has his management interview. Samir gives Deirdre his lucky coin(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kim and Frank's wedding day arrives. Kathy locks herself in the garage with Kim's new car and starts up the engine, while Uncle Harry gets too drunk to give the bride away(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Jack is worried about who will mind Victoria when Sarah starts work. Caroline is worried when Kathy refuses to get out of bed. Frank accuses Eric of stealing Kim's car(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: A beauty queen is targeted by mysterious attackers ahead of the Miss Yorkshire contest, which attracts more than the usual share of attention when busloads of female protesters turn up to picket the event. Ordered to boost his arrest record, Younger finds a novel way of raising the village's crime rate, while Phil spies an opportunity to get closer to Gina. Steven Blakeley and Mark Jordon star(n)

programme.title: The Darling Buds of May
programme.desc: Part two of two. Mariette and Charley locate Pieter and beg him to stay, while Pop faces hostility from the villagers. Primrose does her best to make an impression on the Reverend Candy. David Jason and Pam Ferris star(n)

programme.title: That's My Boy
programme.desc: Ida has a run-in with the law while changing a purchase at the shop(n)

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: Oxford's last-surviving all-female college is holding a reunion to bid farewell to one of its most prominent professors. However, the evening leads to tragedy when a guest is found murdered, and Lewis is convinced the killing is connected to an attack that occurred at the same institution 10 years previously. The detective calls in his former DS, Alison McLennan, to help with the investigation, but the case takes a twist when two more bodies turn up. Crime drama, guest starring Juliet Stevenson and Saskia Reeves, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. The PC's disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, who's frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: The day of the memorial service dawns, a new Dingle arrives and causes a scene at the barn dance. Kathy admits the truth about her relationship with Bernard(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Sarah is worried about leaving Victoria on her first day back at work. Kim tries to organise a honeymoon for herself and Frank. Dolores finishes with Luke(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Deirdre remembers her past marriages and wonders if she is doing the right thing. Ken apologises to her and wishes his ex well. Gail accuses Martin of frightening Nick off(n)

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Steve is annoyed when Liz asks Mike to help him get out of trouble with Christine. Mavis is shocked to discover that Roger has befriended Rita(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: An amateur archaeologist publishes a book about his father's discovery of Celtic artefacts in an ancient mound, and plans a celebration on the site - but landowner Gareth Heldman is less than keen on the idea. programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Walker manages to arrest one member of a robber gang when a sting operation goes wrong - but ends up being taken hostage by the man's brother. The PC's disappearance is bad news for Blaketon, who's frantic to pull together a winning side for the annual cricket match against rivals Scarsdale. Cricketers Martin Bicknell, Ray Illingworth and Brian Close guest star, alongside Dickie Bird as the umpire(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A woman's body is discovered in Captain Hastings' garage on Bonfire Night. It transpires that the deceased was due to marry an MP - and although the evidence suggests suicide, the Belgian detective suspects foul play. David Suchet, Hugh Fraser and Philip Jackson star(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Louisa has problems at school with Toby, Angela Sim's nephew. Meanwhile, Al has a 'Large Whiskey' order and has to ask Bert for some help, and Mrs Tishell seems to be having a delayed onset of mourning. Elsewhere, Martin and Louisa decide to try Mel's suggestions of using amber beads to help James while he is teething(n)

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Penhale decides to have a Police Open day to raise community awareness, but the only genuine visitor is an angry farmer who has had three of her sheep stolen. Meanwhile, Morwenna decides to take Al away for the weekend to try out Hannah's new glamping site, but the trip does not go well as Al accidently burns down the yurt. Elsewhere, James has started biting not only Louisa but other children at the nursery. Louisa speaks to Tina about it, but is surprised to find out Tina has spoken to their lecturer about it too(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: Suspicion falls on members of the immigrant workforce after a suitcase containing the body of farm owner Darren Bailey is pulled from the river. Bill discovers that the farm had been used to launder criminal proceeds through the clever accounting of the victim's much younger Slovenian girlfriend, Lubica, who has been made sole owner of the farm's assets. This makes her a key suspect, particularly after it's revealed that her and Darren had an argument on the night he went missing. However, with many of Darren's employees seeming happy at the news of his demise, and stories coming to light about how he squeezed them at every opportunity, it isn't an open and shut case. Starring Rob Lowe, Rachael Stirling and Angela Griffin(n)

programme.title: Wild Bill
programme.desc: The discovery of a pyre of bodies puts Bill on the hunt for Frank McGill, a hitman notorious for his weapon of choice - a nail-gun. However, having been put into witness protection years earlier after turning in other members of the Maguire gang he worked for, Frank is now off-grid, so Bill sets his sights on tracking him down. Meanwhile, DS Blair is released on bail after information is leaked regarding the unlawful obtaining of key DNA, Commissioner Metcalfe discusses the financial benefits of selling off a disused training facility and the pressure mounts for Bill to resign. Starring Rob Lowe and Rachael Stirling(n)

programme.title: Inspector Morse
programme.desc: The Oxford detective gains a taste of prison life while investigating the murder of an inmate jailed for a property swindle. The victim's two partners-in-crime are also incarcerated in the same low-security cell block. Morse is determined to find out whether they know anything about the killing, but has to avoid offending the prison authorities while also dealing with a brash subordinate. John Thaw and Kevin Whately star, with guest appearances by Sean Bean, Richard Wilson, Jim Broadbent, Diana Quick and Steven Mackintosh(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The death of an unpopular art dealer leaves Poirot with a long list of suspects within his tangled, squabbling family - even though his eccentric wife had predicted his end. Detective drama, starring David Suchet, with Emma Fielding, Fiona Walker and Jeremy Northam(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: A property expert offers advice to British householders aiming to buy a home abroad(n)

programme.title: Clipper: Race of Their Lives

programme.desc: It is January 1936, and diplomat Sir Hallam and Lady Agnes Holland (Ed Stoppard and Keeley Hawes) move into 165 Eaton Place - closely followed by his widowed mother and her wayward sister. programme.title: Worlds Apart with Oksana Boyko
programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.desc: Michael Barrymore hosts the madcap game show, in which three teams of two prove seek to progress along a board game-style path by answering questions. programme.title: Bruce Forsyth's Play Your Cards Right
programme.desc: Game show in which genial host Bruce Forsyth offers contestants the chance to win big cash prizes on the turn of a card(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz show in which pairs of contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions posed to 100 people before the show. At the end of each round, the team with the most points is eliminated until the final two pairs battle for the chance to compete for a cash prize. Presented by Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Coronation Street's Antony Cotton (Sean Tully), co-host of The One Show Alex Jones, writer and broadcaster Gyles Brandreth and Flog It! presenter Paul Martin compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Presented by Bradley Walsh(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Boyzone's Shane Lynch, former athlete Colin Jackson, Emmerdale actress Fiona Wade (Priya Sharma) and comedian Stephen K Amos compete to win thousands of pounds for charity by pitting their general knowledge against one of the show's resident trivia experts. However, they must work as a team to build their prize pot and stay one step ahead of the Chaser. Bradley Walsh hosts(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Solo artist and Atomic Kitten member Liz McClarnon is joined by her family as she competes against TV psychic Uri Geller and his relatives, with £30,000 available to win for their chosen charities, Rasa and British Red Cross respectively. Hosted by Vernon Kay(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Dragons' Den panellist Duncan Bannatyne is joined by his family as he competes against The Bill's Lisa Maxwell (Detective Inspector Samantha Nixon) and her relatives, with £30,000 available to win for their chosen charities, the Scottish Spina Bifida Association and Centrepoint respectively. Vernon Kay hosts(n)

programme.title: All Star Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Coronation Street stars past and present - Keith Duffy (Ciaran McCarthy) and Jennie McAlpine (Fiz Stape) - take part in the game show, teaming up with their relatives to guess the answers to surveys of 100 people in a bid to reach the final round, where they can win up to £30,000 for a charity of their choice. Presented by Vernon Kay(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as comedian Jimmy Carr, Coronation Street's Jack P Shepherd (David Platt), retired rugby union player Matt Dawson and former Big Brother star Sally Bercow pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning thousands of pounds for charity. They must work as a team to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: Chris Tarrant hosts the second of two celebrity specials. Former Spandau Ballet members Tony Hadley and John Keeble take to the hot seats to raise money for The Shooting Stars Children's Hospice, and actress Lynda Bellingham teams up with presenter and conservationist Bill Oddie in an attempt to win cash for children's charity NCH(n)

programme.title: Who Wants to Be a Millionaire?
programme.desc: A classic episode of the big-money quiz where newlywed couples can win up to £1million(n)

programme.title: The Protectors
programme.desc: A man bets his companion he can disappear without trace, and approaches the team to help him do so. Starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: The coroner is outraged when he learns that a wealthy lawyer is likely to get away with a light sentence after running over a pedestrian(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A desperate boxer falsely accused of murdering his wife takes a priest and two of his parishioners hostage. Theo must stop him hurting anyone while also uncovering the real killer(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Two sisters receive unwanted gifts at Christmas - severed human ears. Holmes is enlisted to discover where they came from - and uncovers a disturbing tale of deceit and infidelity. Jeremy Brett stars in his curtain call as the famous detective(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's minicab firm is targeted by saboteurs. However, his partner in the venture is reluctant to involve the police, so he drafts in Terry to act as a driver and help identify the culprits. Needless to say, it soon transpires there is more to the attacks than meets the eye. George Cole, Alfred Burke and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: An amnesiac woman delivers a garbled message about an attack on a diplomatic meeting planned for the following day. She later regains her memory and tells Bodie and Doyle her employers were abducted by the same gunmen who intend to carry out the action. Gordon Jackson, Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Recently released criminal Eddie Boyse becomes prime suspect in an assault case - and Regan believes he is guilty. Unfortunately for the officer, Boyse has the perfect alibi - he was in Regan's company when the crime was committed. Drama, starring John Thaw, with Dennis Waterman and Del Henney(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: A racehorse owner is accused of killing both his champion thoroughbred and its vet, prompting old friend Quincy to track down the real culprit(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Several witnesses overhear a prostitute plotting to murder her lover, but when Theo investigates, he cannot find any trace of the woman in question, or her victim's body(n)

programme.title: The Saint
programme.desc: Templar is asked by the former king of Fedyra to accompany him on an important mission in Switzerland, where he becomes aide to a queen(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur is captivated by an attractive female vocalist, who boasts limited musical talent. Nevertheless, he uses his contacts to secure a gig for her at a club run by a dodgy entertainment manager, and soon both he and Terry find themselves immersed in the seedier side of the showbiz industry during a night's work in a disco. Comedy drama, starring Dennis Waterman and George Cole(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: CI5 investigates the fatal shooting of a South American president's son at a polo match, and finds that the victim's step-sister is unknowingly dating one of the terrorists involved. Ruby Wax and Pierce Brosnan feature in early screen appearances, alongside stars Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan is forced to impose himself on an old girlfriend whose flat is conveniently near a bank that intelligence has revealed is to be raided. Once in position, he and Carter try to pinpoint a radio transmitter that is being used to coordinate the movements of the robbers. Detective drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh returns with the celebrity version of the team quiz show. As ever, four star contestants are challenged to pit their wits against one of the show's ruthless quiz experts, in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds for their charity of choice. This time, Absolute Radio's Christian O'Connell, singer, TV presenter and vocal coach Carrie Grant, Channel 5 children's presenter Naomi Wilkinson and I, Daniel Blake star Dave Johns each try to race down the game board without being caught, before joining forces for the final chase(n)

programme.title: From Russia with Love (1963)
programme.desc: James Bond is sent to steal a top-secret Soviet decoding machine and ends up helping its operator defect to the West. However, he discovers the mission is really a trap set by international crime syndicate Spectre, and struggles to evade his would-be assassins. Spy thriller, starring Sean Connery, Robert Shaw, Daniela Bianchi and Lotte Lenya(n)

programme.title: Fishing Impossible

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: Wild and un-navigable, Africa's iconic Zambezi boasts the world's largest waterfall and deadly rapids. These days, though, its health is under threat. From the delta in Mozambique to the upper reaches in Zambia, the biologist, extreme angler and naturalist investigates why this great river is now in trouble(n)

programme.desc: The team are assigned to thwart the kidnap of a foreign president. Starring Robert Vaughn and Nyree Dawn Porter(n)

programme.desc: A profile of the former football player and manager, who played and managed Ajax and Barcelona, and represented the Netherlands on 48 occasions, scoring 33 goals(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: Mycroft Holmes joins his brother to investigate the murder of a professor's secretary. The only clue is a golden pince-nez found clenched in the hand of the victim. Charles Gray guest stars(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A reclusive lodger's strange behaviour sends his landlady running to Holmes for advice - and what initially seems to be a simple matter turns into a mystery involving an Italian secret society and murder in New York. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett(n)

programme.title: The Memoirs of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A priceless French diamond is stolen from its London display. However, with Holmes missing, Watson and Mycroft are forced to pursue the investigation themselves. Detective drama, guest starring Charles Gray, with Jeremy Brett and Edward Hardwicke(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing: The Opening Show
programme.desc: Oli Bell and the team are joined by Richard Johnson to look ahead to this afternoon's racing at Kempton Park. Plus the latest news ahead of Cheltenham Festival(n)

programme.title: Hornblower
programme.desc: Conclusion. Despite his reservations, Hornblower is reluctant to dishonour his bride-to-be by leaving her standing at the altar. However, with the ink barely dry on the marriage certificate, a surprise guest at the wedding breakfast despatches the groom to France on a mission to uncover the whereabouts of a missing sloop and its captain, Bracegirdle. En route, Hornblower rescues two strangers caught in a storm. Historical Navy drama, starring Ioan Gruffudd, Robert Lindsay, Paul McGann and Lorcan Cranitch(n)

programme.title: River Monsters
programme.desc: Jeremy Wade visits the Alaskan wilderness in search of the Iliamna Lake monster of native legend that is said to drag people down to an icy grave. Subarctic fishing is a first for the extreme angler, who secures his largest catch to date as he pursues the mysterious creature(n)

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of three Cheltenham trials from Kempton Park at 1.15, 1.50, 2.25 and 3.02, followed by a chase at 3.35. Plus, races from 2.05 and 3.15 at Lingfield Park, and 2.45 at Newcastle. With analysis from Richard Johnson, Luke Harvey and Mick FitzGerald, reports by Oli Bell and Alice Plunkett, betting news from Matt Chapman, and commentary by Richard Hoiles(n)

programme.title: Aces High (1976)
programme.desc: A new recruit is proud to join the Royal Flying Corps' elite 76 Squadron under the command of a renowned ace, but is shocked to discover his superior officer has turned to drink as the high fatalities among his men take their toll - with devastating consequences for the pilots. First World War drama, starring Malcolm McDowell, Christopher Plummer, Simon Ward and Peter Firth(n)

programme.title: Spartacus (1960)
programme.desc: A slave trained to fight as a gladiator turns against his masters and leads an army of rebels in a fight for freedom against the Roman state. But as the rebellion gathers momentum, two senators do all they can to exploit the uprising for their own political ends. Stanley Kubrick's epic, starring the late Kirk Douglas, Tony Curtis, Laurence Olivier, Jean Simmons, Charles Laughton and Peter Ustinov, who won the Best Supporting Actor Oscar for his role as the unctuous Roman businessman Lentulus Batiatus(n)

programme.title: How the West Was Won (1962)
programme.desc: Three generations of a pioneering family experience many ordeals and adventures in their endeavour to build a new life for themselves on the American frontier in the 19th century. programme.title: Tony Robinson: Coast to Coast
programme.desc: The actor explores the Yorkshire Dales and in the town of Hawes, meets the team who came together to save local production of Wensleydale cheese by organising a management buy-out of the creamery after the parent company closed it down. Tony also visits the market town of Leyburn on calf auction day, before heading to Aysgarth Falls, a triple flight of waterfalls carved out of the River Ure. In the Swaledale valley, Tony joins in rehearsals of the Muker Silver Band, discovering the origins of this traditional brass outfit(n)

programme.title: The Yorkshire Steam Railway: All Aboard
programme.desc: The return of the behind-the-scenes look at the North York Moors Railway. Piglet's been promoted, but seems to spend more time in his overalls than behind his desk as he grapples with an old American war-horse, the S160 Omaha. Elsewhere on the railway, lovable ticket inspector Bert Blower has a new trainee, and 'king of the carriages' Kieran awaits a Flying Scotsman carriage returning to the railway after being vandalised two years earlier. Plus, there are antics from trainee driver Phil and budget-conscious big boss Chris(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Britain by Barge: Then & Now
programme.desc: Travelling along the Leeds and Liverpool canal, Bill Oddie, Jennie Bond, Anne Diamond and Pete Waterman eventually leave Yorkshire behind them and head into Lancashire. Pete and Anne discover a Polish war memorial and learn about the role canals played in the Second World War. Bill goes in search of an elusive kingfisher, while Jennie walks in Charlotte Brontë's footsteps. Later, Anne samples a Fat Rascal, Pete tries to turn Bill into a steam engine nut and the entire quartet disappear into the dripping darkness of Foulridge Tunnel, where they find out about a famous cow who once swam it(n)

programme.title: The Comeback of Ant McPartlin
programme.desc: Documentary examining the presenter's fall from grace in 2018, when while his marriage to Lisa Armstrong was failing, he was charged with drink-driving and admitted to rehab for a second time. Through interviews with experts, the programme offers an insight into how Ant's world collapsed around him and how it feels to have personal problems played out in front of the nation. It also speculates as to whether Ant will successfully complete his recovery when he rejoins Declan Donnelly for the latest series of Britain's Got Talent(n)

A family from Bristol, who earn enough to be categorised in the wealthiest one per cent in Britain,, trade their safari trip in Zambia for a stay in a B&B on a windswept Isle of Wight, with a family of six from Scunthorpe whose weekly income is roughly £130(n)

programme.title: Husbands from Hell
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on partners who give their wives grief, including Alan in Oxfordshire who turned the family garden into a scrapyard and just cannot stop buying cars - 120 of them at the last count. Jane from Dunfermline has ended up with a Mr Bean lookalike for a husband thanks to her partner Bill, while opera singer Nicky in Bury has to put up with her husband's obsession with cleaning the house at the crack of dawn(n)

programme.title: Hoarders
programme.desc: Decluttering experts Caz and Tee set out to help Plymouth pensioner Arthur, whose two-bedroom flat is packed with piles of junk he cannot bear to throw away. A carpet of newspapers covers every inch of the floor and as a result, family and friends have stopped visiting. This might just be the duo's toughest assignment yet. Meanwhile, Khim Sim is getting married to her fiancé Jim, but first she must clear her hoard so he can move in, and in London, Mandi's passion for vintage clothes is out of control(n)

programme.title: The Woman with 106 Dogs
programme.desc: Documentary about people with large numbers of pets, including a bankrupt woman with over 100 dogs who is struggling to cope now the donations she relies on are starting to run out. A pensioner organises a car boot sale to feed her 30 cats, a reclusive woman keeps 34 retired show dogs, and a couple with over 100 pets make plans for a special living space for the animals(n)

programme.title: The Killing (1956)
programme.desc: An ex-convict assembles a motley crew of crooks and no-hopers to pull off an elaborate robbery at a heavily guarded racetrack. Stanley Kubrick's noir thriller, starring Sterling Hayden, Coleen Gray, Vince Edwards, Jay C Flippen and Timothy Carey(n)

programme.title: Sink the Bismarck! (1960)
programme.desc: Fact-based Second World War drama, charting the mission to track down and eliminate the pride of the German fleet, a fearsome battleship devastating British vessels in the North Atlantic, orchestrated by a British naval officer recovering from his wife's death in an air raid. Starring Kenneth More, Dana Wynter, Laurence Naismith and Karel Stepanek(n)

programme.title: Hell Is for Heroes (1962)
programme.desc: A rebellious soldier defies orders to lead two men on a secret attack on a German pillbox, but his mission fails and the other two soldiers are killed. Court-martialled for his actions, he remains convinced his plan was sound and ignores the sentence to make a second attempt at destroying the emplacement. Second World War adventure, with Steve McQueen, James Coburn and Bobby Darin(n)

programme.title: This Happy Breed (1944)
programme.desc: A working-class family moves into a new suburban home after the father returns from service in the First World War. However, the couple and their children struggle to adjust to life higher up the social ladder. David Lean's drama, adapted by Noel Coward from his play, starring Robert Newton, Celia Johnson, Robert Stanley Holloway and John Mills(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future (1985)
programme.desc: Teenager Marty McFly travels back to the 1950s in a time machine built by his eccentric inventor friend. The return journey proves tricky enough, but he also accidentally alters the sequence of events that led to his parents falling in love - and has to persuade his future dad to win his mother's heart, or he will never be born. Sci-fi comedy adventure, starring Michael J Fox, Christopher Lloyd, Lea Thompson and Crispin Glover(n)

programme.title: Arrival (2016)
programme.desc: The world is thrown into uproar when 12 alien spaceships arrive at locations around the globe. As tensions rise between nations and fear of the arrivals grows, a recently bereaved linguist is recruited by the US army to find a way to communicate with the aliens and discover why they have come. Sci-fi drama, starring Amy Adams, Jeremy Renner and Forest Whitaker(n)

programme.title: The Watch (2012)
programme.desc: A small-town supermarket manager organises a neighbourhood watch group to patrol his community after a suspicious death, but finds the only recruits he can gather do not take the job seriously. They soon face a greater threat than crime when they uncover a secret alien invasion going on under their noses. Sci-fi comedy, starring Ben Stiller, Vince Vaughn, Jonah Hill and Richard Ayoade(n)

programme.title: Northern Soul (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager living in a Northern town in the 1970s finds his horizons expanded when he discovers American soul music. He develops an inseparable friendship with a fellow devotee and the pair hope to achieve a brighter future beyond their bleak daily life. Drama, starring Elliot James Langridge, Josh Whitehouse, Steve Coogan and Ricky Tomlinson(n)

programme.title: Diary of a Wimpy Kid: Dog Days (2012)
programme.desc: Weedy youngster Greg finds his plans for a lazy school holiday ruined when his mother bans video games and his father organises a series of family activities. When he finds out a girl he likes is working at a country club, he tells his family he has a summer job there as an excuse to spend time with her - but struggles to keep up the deception. Family comedy sequel, starring Zachary Gordon and Steve Zahn(n)

programme.title: Megamind (2010)
programme.desc: An evil genius finally succeeds in killing the superhero who has foiled his every evil scheme, but finds life without an enemy boring. To fill the gap, he transforms an ordinary man into a worthy adversary - but when his creation turns evil, it falls to the villain to save the world. Animated comedy, with the voices of Will Ferrell, Tina Fey and Brad Pitt(n)

programme.title: Baby's Day Out (1994)
programme.desc: Three bungling kidnappers hatch a plan to pose as society reporters and snatch the baby son of a wealthy couple. However, the infant escapes on to the streets of New York, evading the crooks' every attempt to recapture him. Comedy, starring Joe Mantegna, Lara Flynn Boyle, Joe Pantoliano and Brian Haley(n)

programme.title: Congo (1995)
programme.desc: An expedition to find King Solomon's diamond mines in the jungles of the Congo disappears without trace. The tycoon behind the mission sends his assistant, a former CIA agent, to find out what became of the explorers - only to discover that the party met a grisly fate at the hands of a horde of vicious apes. Action adventure based on Michael Crichton's novel, with Dylan Walsh, Laura Linney, Tim Curry and Grant Heslov(n)

programme.title: Back to the Future Part II (1989)
programme.desc: Time-travelling teenager Marty McFly travels to the 21st century, where he has to save his children from a terrible fate. However, this proves to have unforeseen consequences when the future version of arch enemy Biff sees a chance to rewrite history for his own benefit, leaving Marty stuck in a twisted parallel universe. Sci-fi comedy sequel, starring Michael J Fox and Christopher Lloyd(n)

programme.title: Commando (1985)
programme.desc: The daughter of retired special forces officer John Matrix is kidnapped by an old comrade-in-arms, now in league with a deposed former dictator. The tyrant plans to use the girl to force Matrix into assassinating the head of the country he once ruled, but is unprepared for the lengths to which the veteran soldier will go to rescue his child. Action adventure, with Arnold Schwarzenegger and Rae Dawn Chong(n)

programme.title: Scouts Guide to the Zombie Apocalypse (2015)
programme.desc: Three boy scouts look forward to spending their last camping trip together. However, they soon have a more pressing need for their wilderness survival skills when their home town is overrun with the walking dead. Fortunately, a tough cocktail waitress is on hand to help the youngsters survive the zombie onslaught. Comedy horror, starring Tye Sheridan, Logan Miller and Joey Morgan(n)

programme.title: Paranormal Activity: The Marked Ones (2014)
programme.desc: A teenager investigates the murder of a neighbour, which leads him to discover evidence of black magic. He begins to experience strange and disturbing changes and realises he may be succumbing to a sinister curse placed on him by a coven of witches and their demonic allies. Horror sequel, starring Andrew Jacobs and Jorge Diaz(n)

programme.title: Red Army (2014)
programme.desc: Documentary about the Soviet Union's ice hockey team, examining how their sporting triumphs reflected the international political situation during the Cold War era. It also tells the story of captain Slava Fetisov, who was hailed as one of the nation's greatest heroes, but came to be regarded as a despised political enemy. In English and Russian(n)

programme.title: Ever After: A Cinderella Story (1998)

Romantic drama, starring Drew Barrymore, Anjelica Huston, Dougray Scott and Timothy West(n)

Comic-book adventure from director Ang Lee, starring Eric Bana, Jennifer Connelly, Sam Elliott and Nick Nolte(n)

Starring Anatoliy Solonitsyn and Ivan Lapikov. In Russian(n)

programme.desc: A boy has to fend for himself when he is accidentally left behind after his family jets off to Paris for Christmas. The youngster initially enjoys his freedom, but is soon forced to resort to ingenious booby traps to defend the family home from two dim-witted burglars. Comedy, starring Macaulay Culkin, Joe Pesci, Daniel Stern, John Heard, Catherine O'Hara and John Candy(n)

programme.title: Dawn of the Planet of the Apes (2014)
programme.desc: A colony of super-intelligent apes thrives following a global virus outbreak that wipes out much of the human race. A survivor tries to make peace with the leader of the apes, but their efforts to reach an understanding are threatened by warmongers on both sides. Sci-fi thriller sequel, starring Andy Serkis, Jason Clarke and Gary Oldman(n)

programme.title: Star Trek: Discovery
programme.desc: Burnham undergoes a merciless mission in the hope of helping Discovery return home, while Tilly works on restoring Stamets' neurofunction(n)

programme.title: Kirstie's Handmade Treasures
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp visits the Chagford Show, Devon, where she enters her home-made elderflower cordial in the garden crafts competition(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Grandparents Alan and Elisa Bowers have lived in Plymouth for all of their lives and are itching for a new start, but are torn between moving to the beautiful Cornwall coast to stay close to family, or relocating to Lanzarote, their dream location. Laura Hamilton shows them three properties in Cornwall for their £300,000 UK budget while Scarlette Douglas has some amazing places in Lanzarote for their budget of £270,000(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun

Jasmine Harman shows them five properties in the west of Barbados for their £300,000 budget(n)

programme.title: Emergency Helicopter Medics
programme.desc: Steph is trapped with multiple injuries after a bad smash near Alnwick in Northumberland, while an accident with a bonfire leaves Paul with serious burns. Meanwhile, in Thetford Forest in Norfolk, the medics have difficulty reaching horse-rider Alice, who has badly broken her ankle(n)

programme.title: 999: On the Front Line
programme.desc: Paramedics in Birmingham try to stop hypothermia from setting in when a woman collapses in the snow. At the same time, Mark and Adi attend to a woman whose waters may have broken at 27 weeks and could be hours away from giving birth. In Coventry, crewmates Neil and Laura treat an elderly man who has a life-threatening infection(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: Billy and Gwendoline rope in Dick to help them put up a chandelier, and then they have to track down some wild boars that are causing chaos in and around the chateau. Clive and Tanith attempt to make a fully self-sufficient dish for their restaurant. Meanwhile at Paul and Karen's chateau, Paul continues the task of converting their water tower into an office - in 33-degree heat(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: David Lewis and Giles Beaton kick off the competition at Langland Road in Swansea, where they hope to impress their guests with an ultra-modern approach. Unfortunately, not all the guests can figure out what the hi-tech offerings are. To make matters worse, traditionalists Gordon and Sandra McCallum are more concerned by cobwebs and dust(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Ruth and Margaret Dugdall welcome their rivals to Castle Lodge Hotel in Felixstowe, Suffolk. Ed Prophet plays a series of tricks on Gordon McCallum in the hope of making him smile, but previous hosts David Lewis and Giles Beaton are unimpressed when the prankster takes on the role of waiter(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Gordon and Sandra McCallum host at Bryn Woodlands House in Colwyn Bay, Conwy, where they take the group harness racing. Ed Prophet worries about getting manure on his face, and later receives a shock. Margaret and Ruth Dugdall think the B&B's decor is poor, and Giles Beaton has a problem with a bottle of ketchup(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: Edward Prophet and fiancee Rachel Pickup play host at the Tollemache Arms in Cheshire as the week's competition enters the final round. The couple pull out all the stops to charm their guests, but their efforts are undermined by a shambolic breakfast service(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: As the contestants prepare to discover which of their establishments has won the competition, the owners of Langland Road are shocked by a peculiar payment, and begin to suspect something is amiss. The Castle Lodge hoteliers are dismayed when criticism is directed towards their new wallpaper, and the proprietors of Bryn Woodland House struggle to face up to their shortcomings(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Thirty seven-year-old elephant Thi is pregnant for the ninth time but the keepers believe she is now three months' overdue. The risks for Thi and her unborn calf are growing by the day, and the staff are worried. Rampant male Rothschild giraffe Meru causes problems for the giraffe herd as he relentlessly chases the females, and male clownfish Polo gets injured while fighting with the other males for the attentions of dominant female Rosie. However, clownfish can change sex at will, and Polo's time alone in an isolation tank produces a surprising side effect(n)

programme.title: The Supervet
programme.desc: Cavachon Stampy has been hit by a car and is in a critical condition, leaving owners Jim and his wife Karen facing a difficult dilemma - to take the chance and put their pet through painful surgery, or let him die. Golden retriever Dylan has a limp, and owner Stuart has driven seven hours from Preston to seek Noel Fitzpatrick's help. Meanwhile, Ginny from Chelsea arrives with her beloved dachshund Lilly-Belle, who has lost the use of her back legs(n)

programme.title: Car S.O.S.
programme.desc: Tim Shaw and Fuzz Townshend travel to Essex, where they rescue a 1974 Chevrolet Corvette Stingray that has been off the road for years. However, they soon discover it may be a bigger job than they anticipated when they get the iconic US vehicle back to the workshop(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau: DIY
programme.desc: At Billy and Gwendoline's chateau, Billy and his father try to level a patio so it can pass health and safety regulations. As Paul and Karen struggle to get the office conversion finished in time, a huge damaged pine tree needs to be cut down(n)

programme.title: Just One Look
programme.desc: Eva now finds herself able to identify several individuals in the photo. Gregory Marsan, a gangster acquaintance, is worried about her safety and asks one of his henchmen to keep an eye on her as she continues her search for Bastien, who is in the power of violent criminal Eric Toussaint. It is becoming clear that Bastien is not the only target as the family home has been bugged and Eva grows increasingly anxious over how to protect her children and who she can trust. Thriller, starring Virginie Ledoyen. In French(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Nine-year-old Tino is rushed into resus with memory problems after slumping over her desk at school. Consultant Liz has difficulty making a diagnosis, suspecting the girl may have a viral infection that causes a swelling of the brain. A woman with a history of anxiety attacks requires a scan after being brought in suffering from numbness down her left side, and an 11-year-old boy is treated for a broken leg after being knocked down by a car(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: A busy night shift sees a series of men admitted with various problems. Father-of-six Stacey is experiencing breathing difficulties, but his demanding attitude appears to mask a vulnerability. Twenty-year-old Sam is brought in after being attacked outside a nightclub and has possible injuries to the neck and spine. He is nervous because his father died at the hospital and, with a broken nose, is worried he may have lost his looks. Henry has an electrician's exam the following morning but has been hiccuping for three days. Registrar Faheem is determined to diagnose the problem and get him well in time(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: The fiery chef tries to change the fortunes of Finn McCool's, a traditional Irish pub in the Hamptons, New York. programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: Food Unwrapped
programme.desc: Kate Quilton learns some important differences between gelato and ice-cream at a parlour in Rome, while Jimmy Doherty visits Mauritius to find out about white and dark rum(n)

programme.desc: Jason and Lindsay from East Yorkshire met 28 years ago when they were working for the RAF. Lindsay suffers from osteoarthritis, which affects her mobility, and as warm sunshine helps her condition their dream is to spend every school holiday in Florida with their son before eventually retiring there. They have a budget of up to £180,000, but will Ben Hillman be able to find them what they're looking for?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Winter Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton shows five properties on Tenerife to newlywed couple Brian and Wendy Woods, who are looking to buy their dream home on the island within a budget of £180,000(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Documentary following life inside one of Britain's busiest A&E departments at King's College Hospital in south London. A doctor is shocked when a young woman arrives in resus with life-threatening swelling on her brain following a random attack by a stranger on the streets, while a mother faces an agonising wait as tests are carried out on her 12-year-old son, who was hit by a car and airlifted to King's by emergency medics. A 90-year-old former circus performer is also in A&E after collapsing at home, and news of his colourful past quickly circulates among the staff(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Eighty-year-old Rose has fluid on her lungs and is having trouble breathing. Also in resus at King's College Hospital is Kevin, a 55-year-old trucker who has sustained injuries in a road accident(n)

programme.title: Father Ted

programme.desc: The priest makes a valiant effort to give up cigarettes for Lent, inspired by a challenge from Dick Byrne, his opposite number over on Rugged Island, but soon finds he is not cut out for a life of self-denial. Comedy, starring Dermot Morgan and Ardal O'Hanlon(n)

The show features first-hand accounts from the people who were making telly at the time, including Barry Cryer, Janet Street-Porter and Bill Oddie(n)

On arrival, the guests are wowed by the interior and artwork on show. However, Luke and John discover some grime underneath the glamour and Debbie is concerned about her traditional Chinese bed. At dinner, all the guests congratulate Mark and Heidi on their pretty property, but things soon turn ugly when talk turns to the looming payment day as it becomes clear that the B&B'ers have grievances to air(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me

She has been pursuing her dream of becoming a film star, and is ready to snag a guest part on popular US soap General Hospital. Later, the pair plan a hip-hop tour of LA in a 'lowrider', and go undercover with a private detective, as Romesh fulfills his fantasy of reliving Beverly Hills Cop. programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson, Richard Hammond and James May embark on a race from London's Wembley Stadium to Milan's San Siro, the home of Italian football giants AC and Inter. programme.desc: The comedian visits countries that are rarely visited by tourists to get a sense of the local culture and find out if they deserve their reputation. His first destination is Haiti, a country mostly famous for a dictatorship and a series of natural disasters. Accompanied by journalist Jeremy Dupin, he visits the iron market, attends vodou ceremonies, and explores a cathedral destroyed by an earthquake(n)

programme.title: Slow Train Through Africa with Griff Rhys Jones
programme.desc: On the final leg of his journey, the comedian crosses 1,000 miles of South Africa, reaching the most southerly passenger railway station on the continent. Heading to Johannesburg, he reveals how the city has benefited from the scale of gold-mining in the area. Later on, he takes to the skies in a light aircraft for a bird's-eye view of one of South Africa's largest ostrich farms, and catches one of the world's most luxurious trains for a seven-hour ride to Cape Town, before reaching the coastal settlement of Simon's Town(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey profiles two of Britain's greatest wartime leaders. He begins by revealing how Winston Churchill came to write a biography of his ancestor John, who led the nation against Louis XIV of France. Starkey also explores how preparations for the Second World War were affected by the prime minister's knowledge of his 17th-century forebear's achievements(n)

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey explains how writing a biography of his ancestor John, the first Duke of Marlborough, changed the way Winston Churchill was seen by the public and politicians, and so paved the way for his return to power. The first volume, published in 1933, reinforced the perception of him as a man past his time. But it was the second instalment, depicting John Churchill's march to the Danube and victory at Blenheim, which began to mark out Winston as the only leading politician who truly knew war(n)

programme.title: Secret Nazi Bases
programme.desc: A mile-and-a half of recently-excavated trenches, bunkers, barracks, and gun encasements near Pointe du Hoc, which tell a new story of D-Day(n)

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: Steam Train Britain

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: A tour of the mightiest rivers in the world, from the deep waters of Siberia's Lake Baikal, home to the world's only freshwater seal, to the wetlands downstream of Earth's tallest waterfall patrolled by frenzied piranhas. There is also footage of the lightning-quick ambush of wildebeest by crocodiles in Africa and macaque monkeys swimming underwater to catch crabs in Indonesia. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: David Attenborough looks at the world's caves, including the Lechuguilla Cave in America, which contains chambers filled with 6m-long crystals. He observes creatures in Texas and Thailand that never see daylight, and inside Deer Cave in Borneo he finds five million bats whose droppings support an entire ecosystem(n)

programme.title: Planet Earth
programme.desc: The nature series looks at animals that live on some of the world's highest mountains. Cameras show gelada baboons screaming to frighten off predators in Ethiopia, demoiselle cranes struggling to migrate over the Himalayas, snow leopards hunting on the peaks of Pakistan and a wild giant panda nursing a baby in a hillside cave. Presented by David Attenborough(n)

programme.title: Chris Tarrant: Extreme Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Broadcaster Chris Tarrant continues his travels along the world's most challenging railways. Here he attempts to cross the Andes, setting off from the coast of Chile and heading for the Bolivian capital of Sucre. Along the way he traverses some of the driest and most inhospitable environments on the planet, hitches rides on unusual trains and deals with altitude sickness at more than 3,000 metres(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: How in devising the F35 Lightnin II, engineers have pushed the boundaries of aviation technology further than ever before and created a brand new, revolutionary, fighter aircraft capable of handling threats in the air and on the ground. Plus, Rob Collings flies one of the most successful aircraft of the Second World War, the distinctive P38, former RAF test pilot Andy Edgell tracks down the product of a top secret research project, the X-14 and retired US Air Force pilot Mace Carpenter reconnects with the stealthy F117(n)

programme.title: The Churchills

programme.title: The Churchills
programme.desc: Historian David Starkey describes how Winston Churchill drew on the lessons of his ancestor John to lead the UK through the Second World War. At two key moments, the prime minister's strategic sense helped avert disaster, and in both decisions, the influence of the Duke of Marlborough is apparent. Starkey argues that it was Winston's ability to control events by giving them a narrative meaning that enabled him to shape world history long after his fall from power(n)

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson embarks on another expedition through some of Britain's most historic landscapes. The moors and valleys of West Yorkshire were the home of, and inspiration for, the Brontes, the literary family who produced such classics as Wuthering Heights and Jane Eyre. Over four days, Tony heads out from the Victorian wool capital of Bradford and treks in a giant loop around what is now known as Bronte Country. From the birth of Charlotte, Emily, Anne and their brother Branwell in the suburb of Thornton, he traces their childhoods to the much-romanticised Bronte hub of Haworth, finding that the harsh reality of life in the 1820s was far removed from the fantasy world dreamed up by the children at their father's parsonage home(n)

programme.desc: Henry Ford's plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length(n)

programme.desc: It is the second night of the competition in and around Newcastle, and art teacher Karen's turn to host. She's looking to impress her guests with a ginger-themed menu. Software salesman Mark winds Debs up about 64-year-old ladies' man David, who likes to shock the group with his antics in Ibiza(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: Mobile hairdresser and amateur musical songstress Debbie hosts the third dinner party in and around Newcastle, hoping to inject a little showbiz sparkle to her night(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: The fourth host in Newcastle is 64-year-old David, who aims to win with an elaborate menu of Ibizan-style tapas and paella. But he may have bitten off more than he can chew(n)

programme.title: Come Dine with Me
programme.desc: It's the final night from in and around Newcastle, and the turn of Essex boy Mark to impress his new Geordie chums with a menu of deep fried brie, slow-cooked lamb and a passion fruit pavlova. Debs, again, struggles with the menu but also lets out her personal feelings about the group. Art teacher Karen gets it in the neck for ruining other people's nights, Mark is accused of being boring, and David settles for bringing a very weird noise to the table(n)

programme.desc: Jim and Justine kick off the competition in Portsmouth, before Leanne and Gary and Stewart and Lynn take their turns as they try to win the £1,000 prize(n)

With five options lined up for their £120,000 budget, will they find what they're looking for?(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: North Yorkshire couple Carol and Steve are searching for a holiday home in Oliva, eastern Spain, so Danni Menzies shows them five properties for their £80,000 budget(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun
programme.desc: Laura Hamilton is house-hunting in the French countryside around Limoges with a Norfolk couple who want a place to retire that is big enough to accommodate their large family(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads for Boston to help change the fortunes of a failing Italian restaurant run by two brothers. The eatery's decline began when one of the pair started embezzling money to fund his prescription drug addiction, and things only got worse when the he was sentenced to four years in prison. With the time now served, he has returned, but must first regain his brother's trust before he can save their business(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay travels to Fremont, California, in an attempt to save a restaurant that is tearing the owners' marriage apart. Saki Kavouniaris took over Spin a Yarn in 1995 and began to turn it into a success, before marrying bartender Jennifer. When the business started to fail, the couple decided to splash out on refurbishments to recover their glory days, but their efforts have been futile and now their relationship is at stake(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Chef Gordon Ramsay heads to Charlie's Italian bistro in La Verne, southern California, where the owner's lack of business nous is putting her relatives' homes at risk, after her mother and sister put them up as collateral. To make matters worse, the restaurant's food is less than appetising and its long-serving chef seems to be incompetent(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay heads to Baltimore, Maryland, where Denise Whiting has alienated customers by trademarking the word 'hon', a term of endearment used by the local community. When she opened Cafe Hon in 1992 it was a roaring success, but as a result of Denise's unwavering business ambition, people started to boycott the restaurant, leaving her frustrated by falling sales(n)

programme.title: Ramsay's Kitchen Nightmares USA
programme.desc: Gordon Ramsay's next stop is Philadelphia, where family-run restaurant Chiarella's is struggling to survive in an area full of thriving Italian eateries. programme.desc: An examination of the turbulent relationship between Diana, Princess of Wales and her formidable stepmother Raine Spencer, who rose from humble origins with the help of her larger-than-life mother, the novelist Barbara Cartland, to become a countess and a member of the royal family. Raine was a divisive figure, who was doted on by her husband Johnnie Spencer but loathed by Diana and her siblings. Yet as the princess's marriage collapsed, she reached out to her stepmother for support, marking a change in the women's relationship(n)
